<type>file</type>
<content>1: package main
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"log"
5: 	"os"
6: 	"path/filepath"
7: 	"strings"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/verify"
10: )
11: 
12: var listOpenIssues = verify.ListOpenIssues
13: var listOpenIssuesWithDir = verify.ListOpenIssuesWithDir
14: 
15: // investigationDirCache holds pre-loaded directory listings for investigation discovery.
16: // This prevents O(n^2) behavior when discovering investigation paths for many agents.
17: // Without this cache, each agent would call os.ReadDir() 2-3 times on directories
18: // with 500+ files, resulting in 300+ agents x 500+ files x 2 calls = massive slowdown.
19: type investigationDirCache struct {
20: 	// entries maps directory path -> list of .md file names (not full DirEntry, just names for efficiency)
21: 	entries map[string][]string
22: }
23: 
24: // buildInvestigationDirCache pre-loads directory listings for investigation discovery.
25: // Call this once before processing agents, then pass to discoverInvestigationPath.
26: func buildInvestigationDirCache(projectDirs []string) *investigationDirCache {
27: 	cache := &investigationDirCache{
28: 		entries: make(map[string][]string),
29: 	}
30: 
31: 	for _, projectDir := range projectDirs {
32: 		if projectDir == "" {
33: 			continue
34: 		}
35: 
36: 		// Cache .kb/investigations/
37: 		investigationsDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".kb", "investigations")
38: 		if entries, err := os.ReadDir(investigationsDir); err == nil {
39: 			var mdFiles []string
40: 			for _, entry := range entries {
41: 				if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md") {
42: 					mdFiles = append(mdFiles, entry.Name())
43: 				}
44: 			}
45: 			cache.entries[investigationsDir] = mdFiles
46: 		}
47: 
48: 		// Cache .kb/investigations/simple/
49: 		simpleDir := filepath.Join(investigationsDir, "simple")
50: 		if entries, err := os.ReadDir(simpleDir); err == nil {
51: 			var mdFiles []string
52: 			for _, entry := range entries {
53: 				if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md") {
54: 					mdFiles = append(mdFiles, entry.Name())
55: 				}
56: 			}
57: 			cache.entries[simpleDir] = mdFiles
58: 		}
59: 	}
60: 
61: 	return cache
62: }
63: 
64: // getEntries returns cached directory entries, or empty slice if not cached.
65: func (c *investigationDirCache) getEntries(dirPath string) []string {
66: 	if c == nil || c.entries == nil {
67: 		return nil
68: 	}
69: 	return c.entries[dirPath]
70: }
71: 
72: // discoverInvestigationPath attempts to find an investigation file for an agent
73: // using a fallback chain when the agent hasn't reported an investigation_path via beads comment.
74: //
75: // IMPORTANT: Pass a pre-built investigationDirCache to avoid O(n^2) directory scanning.
76: // Without the cache, this function would call os.ReadDir() for each agent, causing
77: // massive slowdowns with 300+ agents and 500+ investigation files.
78: //
79: // Fallback chain:
80: // 1. Search .kb/investigations/ for files matching workspace name pattern
81: // 2. Search .kb/investigations/ for files matching beads ID
82: // 3. Check workspace directory for investigation .md files (excluding SPAWN_CONTEXT.md and SYNTHESIS.md)
83: func discoverInvestigationPath(workspaceName, beadsID, projectDir string, cache *investigationDirCache) string {
84: 	if projectDir == "" {
85: 		return ""
86: 	}
87: 
88: 	// Extract keywords from workspace name for matching (e.g., "og-inv-skillc-deploy-06jan-ed96" -> "skillc-deploy")
89: 	// Workspace names follow pattern: {project}-{skill}-{topic}-{date}-{hash}
90: 	workspaceKeywords := extractWorkspaceKeywords(workspaceName)
91: 
92: 	investigationsDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".kb", "investigations")
93: 
94: 	// Use cached entries if available (O(1) lookup vs O(n) ReadDir)
95: 	entries := cache.getEntries(investigationsDir)
96: 	if entries == nil {
97: 		// Fallback to direct read if not cached (shouldn't happen in normal use)
98: 		if dirEntries, err := os.ReadDir(investigationsDir); err == nil {
99: 			for _, entry := range dirEntries {
100: 				if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md") {
101: 					entries = append(entries, entry.Name())
102: 				}
103: 			}
104: 		}
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	// 1. Search for files matching beads ID (e.g., "orch-go-51jz" in filename)
108: 	// This is the most specific match and should be checked first.
109: 	if beadsID != "" {
110: 		// Extract short ID from beads ID (last segment after -)
111: 		shortID := beadsID
112: 		if idx := strings.LastIndex(beadsID, "-"); idx != -1 && idx < len(beadsID)-1 {
113: 			shortID = beadsID[idx+1:]
114: 		}
115: 
116: 		for _, name := range entries {
117: 			// Check if filename contains beads ID or short ID
118: 			if strings.Contains(name, beadsID) || strings.Contains(name, shortID) {
119: 				return filepath.Join(investigationsDir, name)
120: 			}
121: 		}
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	// 2. Search .kb/investigations/ for files matching workspace name pattern
125: 	// Workspace names are specific to the agent's task.
126: 	// We reverse the entries list to find the most recent files first (since they are date-prefixed).
127: 	reversedEntries := make([]string, len(entries))
128: 	for i, name := range entries {
129: 		reversedEntries[len(entries)-1-i] = name
130: 	}
131: 
132: 	// First pass: look for exact topic match (highest confidence)
133: 	// We now require at least one keyword match, but we prefer files that match MORE keywords.
134: 	var bestMatch string
135: 	maxMatches := 0
136: 
137: 	for _, name := range reversedEntries {
138: 		matches := 0
139: 		for _, keyword := range workspaceKeywords {
140: 			if keyword != "" && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(name), strings.ToLower(keyword)) {
141: 				matches++
142: 			}
143: 		}
144: 
145: 		if matches > maxMatches {
146: 			maxMatches = matches
147: 			bestMatch = filepath.Join(investigationsDir, name)
148: 			// If we match all keywords, return immediately (highest confidence)
149: 			if matches == len(workspaceKeywords) && len(workspaceKeywords) > 0 {
150: 				return bestMatch
151: 			}
152: 		}
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	if bestMatch != "" {
156: 		return bestMatch
157: 	}
158: 
159: 	// 3. Search for simpler investigations or workspace-specific ones
160: 	if beadsID != "" {
161: 		// Also check .kb/investigations/simple/ (for simpler investigations)
162: 		simpleDir := filepath.Join(investigationsDir, "simple")
163: 		simpleEntries := cache.getEntries(simpleDir)
164: 		if simpleEntries == nil {
165: 			// Fallback to direct read if not cached
166: 			if dirEntries, err := os.ReadDir(simpleDir); err == nil {
167: 				for _, entry := range dirEntries {
168: 					if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md") {
169: 						simpleEntries = append(simpleEntries, entry.Name())
170: 					}
171: 				}
172: 			}
173: 		}
174: 
175: 		for _, name := range simpleEntries {
176: 			for _, keyword := range workspaceKeywords {
177: 				if keyword != "" && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(name), strings.ToLower(keyword)) {
178: 					return filepath.Join(simpleDir, name)
179: 				}
180: 			}
181: 		}
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	// 4. Check workspace directory for investigation .md files
185: 	// This is per-workspace so not cached (each workspace is different)
186: 	workspaceDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "workspace", workspaceName)
187: 	if wsEntries, err := os.ReadDir(workspaceDir); err == nil {
188: 		for _, entry := range wsEntries {
189: 			if entry.IsDir() {
190: 				continue
191: 			}
192: 			name := entry.Name()
193: 			// Skip standard workspace files
194: 			if name == "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md" || name == "SYNTHESIS.md" || name == "ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md" ||
195: 				name == "SESSION_HANDOFF.md" || name == "AGENT_MANIFEST.json" || name == "VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml" ||
196: 				name == ".session_id" || name == ".spawn_time" ||
197: 				name == ".tier" || name == ".beads_id" || name == ".spawn_mode" {
198: 				continue
199: 			}
200: 			// Check for .md files that might be investigation files
201: 			if strings.HasSuffix(name, ".md") && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(name), "inv") {
202: 				return filepath.Join(workspaceDir, name)
203: 			}
204: 		}
205: 	}
206: 
207: 	return ""
208: }
209: 
210: // extractWorkspaceKeywords extracts meaningful keywords from a workspace name for investigation matching.
211: // Workspace names follow pattern: {project}-{skill}-{topic}-{date}-{hash}
212: // Example: "og-inv-skillc-deploy-06jan-ed96" -> ["skillc", "deploy"]
213: func extractWorkspaceKeywords(workspaceName string) []string {
214: 	parts := strings.Split(workspaceName, "-")
215: 	if len(parts) < 3 {
216: 		return nil
217: 	}
218: 
219: 	var keywords []string
220: 
221: 	// Skip prefix parts that are likely project or skill markers
222: 	skipPrefixes := []string{"og", "inv", "feat", "fix", "debug", "audit", "impl", "arch", "research"}
223: 	prefixSet := make(map[string]bool)
224: 	for _, p := range skipPrefixes {
225: 		prefixSet[p] = true
226: 	}
227: 
228: 	for _, part := range parts {
229: 		// Skip short parts (likely hash or date)
230: 		if len(part) <= 2 {
231: 			continue
232: 		}
233: 		// Skip parts that look like dates (e.g., "06jan", "2026")
234: 		if len(part) == 5 && strings.Contains(part, "jan") || strings.Contains(part, "feb") ||
235: 			strings.Contains(part, "mar") || strings.Contains(part, "apr") ||
236: 			strings.Contains(part, "may") || strings.Contains(part, "jun") ||
237: 			strings.Contains(part, "jul") || strings.Contains(part, "aug") ||
238: 			strings.Contains(part, "sep") || strings.Contains(part, "oct") ||
239: 			strings.Contains(part, "nov") || strings.Contains(part, "dec") {
240: 			continue
241: 		}
242: 		// Skip common prefixes
243: 		if prefixSet[strings.ToLower(part)] {
244: 			continue
245: 		}
246: 		// Skip parts that look like short hashes (4 hex chars at end)
247: 		if len(part) == 4 && isHexLike(part) {
248: 			continue
249: 		}
250: 		keywords = append(keywords, part)
251: 	}
252: 
253: 	return keywords
254: }
255: 
256: // isHexLike returns true if the string looks like a short hex hash (all lowercase letters/digits).
257: func isHexLike(s string) bool {
258: 	for _, c := range s {
259: 		if !((c >= 'a' && c <= 'f') || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')) {
260: 			return false
261: 		}
262: 	}
263: 	return true
264: }
265: 
266: // uniqueProjectDirs deduplicates project directories while preserving order.
267: func uniqueProjectDirs(dirs []string) []string {
268: 	seen := make(map[string]bool, len(dirs))
269: 	result := make([]string, 0, len(dirs))
270: 	for _, dir := range dirs {
271: 		if dir == "" {
272: 			continue
273: 		}
274: 		cleaned := filepath.Clean(dir)
275: 		if seen[cleaned] {
276: 			continue
277: 		}
278: 		seen[cleaned] = true
279: 		result = append(result, cleaned)
280: 	}
281: 	return result
282: }
283: 
284: // listActiveIssues retrieves active issues (open or in_progress) across project directories.
285: // Returns a map of beadsID -> Issue, plus beadsID -> projectDir for cross-project lookups.
286: //
287: // This includes both "open" and "in_progress" statuses because:
288: // - Auto-created issues (spawn without --issue) may not get updated to in_progress
289: // - The UpdateIssueStatus call can fail silently during spawn
290: // - Filtering only in_progress causes newly spawned agents to be invisible in the dashboard
291: //   while orch status (which uses workspace/session discovery) can see them
292: func listActiveIssues(projectDirs []string) (map[string]*verify.Issue, map[string]string) {
293: 	issues := make(map[string]*verify.Issue)
294: 	projectDirsByID := make(map[string]string)
295: 
296: 	if len(projectDirs) == 0 {
297: 		openIssues, err := listOpenIssues()
298: 		if err != nil {
299: 			log.Printf("Warning: failed to list open issues: %v", err)
300: 			return issues, projectDirsByID
301: 		}
302: 
303: 		for id, issue := range openIssues {
304: 			status := strings.ToLower(issue.Status)
305: 			if status == "open" || status == "in_progress" {
306: 				issues[id] = issue
307: 			}
308: 		}
309: 
310: 		return issues, projectDirsByID
311: 	}
312: 
313: 	for _, projectDir := range projectDirs {
314: 		openIssues, err := listOpenIssuesWithDir(projectDir)
315: 		if err != nil {
316: 			log.Printf("Warning: failed to list open issues for %s: %v", projectDir, err)
317: 			continue
318: 		}
319: 
320: 		for id, issue := range openIssues {
321: 			status := strings.ToLower(issue.Status)
322: 			if status == "open" || status == "in_progress" {
323: 				if _, exists := issues[id]; !exists {
324: 					issues[id] = issue
325: 					projectDirsByID[id] = projectDir
326: 				}
327: 			}
328: 		}
329: 	}
330: 
331: 	return issues, projectDirsByID
332: }

(End of file - total 332 lines)
</content>

part.text:
**Evaluating CLI command restructuring**

The current sessions command querying OpenCode history conflicts with the new requirement for "orch sessions" to query live sessions via opencode.ListSessions filtered by metadata. I'm considering replacing or renaming the existing sessions command (e.g., to "orch history") and making the new "orch sessions" the live query command, aligning with the requirement and two-lane split.

**Checking for existing history command**

I'm verifying if an "orch history" command already exists since migrating old sessions there might be needed to avoid breaking, even though migration isn't in the scope.

state.output: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/cmd/orch/history.go

state.title: cmd/orch/history.go
state.output:
<path>/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/cmd/orch/history.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: // history.go - Show agent history with skill usage analytics
2: package main
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"encoding/json"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"os"
8: 	"path/filepath"
9: 	"regexp"
10: 	"sort"
11: 	"strings"
12: 	"time"
13: 
14: 	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
15: )
16: 
17: var (
18: 	// History command flags
19: 	historyDays       int
20: 	historyJSONOutput bool
21: 	historyProject    string
22: 
23: 	// Pre-compiled regex patterns for history.go
24: 	regexSkillGuidance   = regexp.MustCompile(`## SKILL GUIDANCE \(([a-z0-9-]+)\)`)
25: 	regexSkillField      = regexp.MustCompile(`(?:^|\n)(?:\*\*|\-\s+)?[Ss]kill:(?:\*\*)?\s*([a-z0-9-]+)`)
26: 	regexSkillUsing      = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)Using\s+([a-z0-9-]+)\s+skill:`)
27: 	regexPhaseField      = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^\*\*Phase:\*\*\s*(\w+)`)
28: 	regexStartedField    = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^\*\*Started:\*\*\s*(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})`)
29: 	regexLastUpdated     = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^\*\*Last Updated:\*\*\s*(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})`)
30: )
31: 
32: var historyCmd = &cobra.Command{
33: 	Use:   "history",
34: 	Short: "Show agent history with skill usage analytics",
35: 	Long: `Show agent history with skill usage analytics.
36: 
37: Analyzes workspace files to extract:
38: - Skill usage patterns
39: - Success rates by skill
40: - Adoption metrics
41: 
42: Examples:
43:   orch history                  # Show last 30 days
44:   orch history --days 7         # Show last 7 days
45:   orch history --json           # Output as JSON`,
46: 	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
47: 		return runHistory()
48: 	},
49: }
50: 
51: func init() {
52: 	historyCmd.Flags().IntVar(&historyDays, "days", 30, "Number of days to analyze")
53: 	historyCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&historyJSONOutput, "json", false, "Output as JSON")
54: 	historyCmd.Flags().StringVar(&historyProject, "project", "", "Project directory (default: current dir)")
55: 
56: 	rootCmd.AddCommand(historyCmd)
57: }
58: 
59: // SkillUsage represents usage of a skill in a workspace.
60: type SkillUsage struct {
61: 	SkillName     string     `json:"skill_name"`
62: 	WorkspaceName string     `json:"workspace_name"`
63: 	WorkspacePath string     `json:"workspace_path"`
64: 	Phase         string     `json:"phase,omitempty"`
65: 	Started       *time.Time `json:"started,omitempty"`
66: 	Completed     *time.Time `json:"completed,omitempty"`
67: 	Success       bool       `json:"success"`
68: }
69: 
70: // SkillStats represents aggregated statistics for a skill.
71: type SkillStats struct {
72: 	SkillName      string   `json:"name"`
73: 	TotalUses      int      `json:"total_uses"`
74: 	SuccessfulUses int      `json:"successful_uses"`
75: 	FailedUses     int      `json:"failed_uses"`
76: 	SuccessRate    float64  `json:"success_rate"`
77: 	Workspaces     []string `json:"workspaces"`
78: }
79: 
80: // SkillAnalytics represents complete skill usage analytics.
81: type SkillAnalytics struct {
82: 	TotalWorkspaces         int                   `json:"total_workspaces"`
83: 	WorkspacesWithSkills    int                   `json:"workspaces_with_skills"`
84: 	WorkspacesWithoutSkills int                   `json:"workspaces_without_skills"`
85: 	SkillAdoptionRate       float64               `json:"skill_adoption_rate"`
86: 	DateRange               *DateRange            `json:"date_range,omitempty"`
87: 	Skills                  []SkillStats          `json:"skills"`
88: }
89: 
90: // DateRange represents a date range.
91: type DateRange struct {
92: 	Start string `json:"start,omitempty"`
93: 	End   string `json:"end,omitempty"`
94: }
95: 
96: func runHistory() error {
97: 	// Determine project directory
98: 	projectDir := historyProject
99: 	if projectDir == "" {
100: 		var err error
101: 		projectDir, err = os.Getwd()
102: 		if err != nil {
103: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to get current directory: %w", err)
104: 		}
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	// Analyze skill usage
108: 	analytics := analyzeSkillUsage(projectDir, historyDays)
109: 
110: 	if historyJSONOutput {
111: 		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(analytics, "", "  ")
112: 		if err != nil {
113: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal JSON: %w", err)
114: 		}
115: 		fmt.Println(string(data))
116: 		return nil
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	// Format human-readable output
120: 	output := formatSkillAnalytics(analytics)
121: 	fmt.Println(output)
122: 	return nil
123: }
124: 
125: func analyzeSkillUsage(projectDir string, days int) *SkillAnalytics {
126: 	workspaceDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "workspace")
127: 
128: 	// Scan for skill usages
129: 	usages := scanWorkspacesForSkills(workspaceDir, days)
130: 
131: 	// Aggregate statistics
132: 	statsBySkill := aggregateSkillStats(usages)
133: 
134: 	// Count total workspaces
135: 	totalWorkspaces := 0
136: 	if entries, err := os.ReadDir(workspaceDir); err == nil {
137: 		for _, entry := range entries {
138: 			if entry.IsDir() && !strings.HasPrefix(entry.Name(), ".") {
139: 				totalWorkspaces++
140: 			}
141: 		}
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	workspacesWithSkills := len(usages)
145: 	workspacesWithoutSkills := totalWorkspaces - workspacesWithSkills
146: 
147: 	// Calculate adoption rate
148: 	adoptionRate := 0.0
149: 	if totalWorkspaces > 0 {
150: 		adoptionRate = float64(workspacesWithSkills) / float64(totalWorkspaces) * 100
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	// Calculate date range
154: 	var dateRange *DateRange
155: 	var minDate, maxDate time.Time
156: 	for _, usage := range usages {
157: 		if usage.Started != nil {
158: 			if minDate.IsZero() || usage.Started.Before(minDate) {
159: 				minDate = *usage.Started
160: 			}
161: 			if maxDate.IsZero() || usage.Started.After(maxDate) {
162: 				maxDate = *usage.Started
163: 			}
164: 		}
165: 	}
166: 	if !minDate.IsZero() {
167: 		dateRange = &DateRange{
168: 			Start: minDate.Format("2006-01-02"),
169: 			End:   maxDate.Format("2006-01-02"),
170: 		}
171: 	}
172: 
173: 	// Convert stats map to sorted slice
174: 	var skillStats []SkillStats
175: 	for _, stats := range statsBySkill {
176: 		skillStats = append(skillStats, stats)
177: 	}
178: 	sort.Slice(skillStats, func(i, j int) bool {
179: 		return skillStats[i].TotalUses > skillStats[j].TotalUses
180: 	})
181: 
182: 	return &SkillAnalytics{
183: 		TotalWorkspaces:         totalWorkspaces,
184: 		WorkspacesWithSkills:    workspacesWithSkills,
185: 		WorkspacesWithoutSkills: workspacesWithoutSkills,
186: 		SkillAdoptionRate:       adoptionRate,
187: 		DateRange:               dateRange,
188: 		Skills:                  skillStats,
189: 	}
190: }
191: 
192: func scanWorkspacesForSkills(workspaceDir string, days int) []SkillUsage {
193: 	if _, err := os.Stat(workspaceDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
194: 		return nil
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	var usages []SkillUsage
198: 	cutoffDate := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -days)
199: 
200: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(workspaceDir)
201: 	if err != nil {
202: 		return nil
203: 	}
204: 
205: 	for _, entry := range entries {
206: 		if !entry.IsDir() || strings.HasPrefix(entry.Name(), ".") {
207: 			continue
208: 		}
209: 
210: 		workspacePath := filepath.Join(workspaceDir, entry.Name())
211: 		usage := extractSkillFromWorkspace(workspacePath, entry.Name())
212: 
213: 		if usage != nil {
214: 			// Apply date filter
215: 			if usage.Started != nil && usage.Started.Before(cutoffDate) {
216: 				continue
217: 			}
218: 			usages = append(usages, *usage)
219: 		}
220: 	}
221: 
222: 	return usages
223: }
224: 
225: func extractSkillFromWorkspace(workspacePath, workspaceName string) *SkillUsage {
226: 	// Try both WORKSPACE.md (legacy) and SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
227: 	var content []byte
228: 	var err error
229: 
230: 	spawnContextPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md")
231: 	workspaceFilePath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "WORKSPACE.md")
232: 
233: 	if _, err = os.Stat(spawnContextPath); err == nil {
234: 		content, err = os.ReadFile(spawnContextPath)
235: 	} else if _, err = os.Stat(workspaceFilePath); err == nil {
236: 		content, err = os.ReadFile(workspaceFilePath)
237: 	} else {
238: 		return nil
239: 	}
240: 
241: 	if err != nil {
242: 		return nil
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	contentStr := string(content)
246: 
247: 	// Extract skill name - multiple patterns
248: 	var skillName string
249: 
250: 	// Pattern 1: "## SKILL GUIDANCE (skill-name)"
251: 	if match := regexSkillGuidance.FindStringSubmatch(contentStr); match != nil {
252: 		skillName = match[1]
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	// Pattern 2: "**Skill:**", "- Skill:", "Skill:"
256: 	if skillName == "" {
257: 		if match := regexSkillField.FindStringSubmatch(contentStr); match != nil {
258: 			skillName = match[1]
259: 		}
260: 	}
261: 
262: 	// Pattern 3: "Using skill-name skill:"
263: 	if skillName == "" {
264: 		if match := regexSkillUsing.FindStringSubmatch(contentStr); match != nil {
265: 			skillName = match[1]
266: 		}
267: 	}
268: 
269: 	if skillName == "" {
270: 		return nil
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	// Extract phase
274: 	var phase string
275: 	if match := regexPhaseField.FindStringSubmatch(contentStr); match != nil {
276: 		phase = match[1]
277: 	}
278: 
279: 	// Extract started date
280: 	var started *time.Time
281: 	if match := regexStartedField.FindStringSubmatch(contentStr); match != nil {
282: 		if t, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", match[1]); err == nil {
283: 			started = &t
284: 		}
285: 	}
286: 
287: 	// Check success (Phase: Complete)
288: 	success := strings.EqualFold(phase, "complete")
289: 
290: 	// Extract completion date
291: 	var completed *time.Time
292: 	if success {
293: 		if match := regexLastUpdated.FindStringSubmatch(contentStr); match != nil {
294: 			if t, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", match[1]); err == nil {
295: 				completed = &t
296: 			}
297: 		}
298: 	}
299: 
300: 	return &SkillUsage{
301: 		SkillName:     skillName,
302: 		WorkspaceName: workspaceName,
303: 		WorkspacePath: workspacePath,
304: 		Phase:         phase,
305: 		Started:       started,
306: 		Completed:     completed,
307: 		Success:       success,
308: 	}
309: }
310: 
311: func aggregateSkillStats(usages []SkillUsage) map[string]SkillStats {
312: 	statsMap := make(map[string]SkillStats)
313: 
314: 	for _, usage := range usages {
315: 		stats, ok := statsMap[usage.SkillName]
316: 		if !ok {
317: 			stats = SkillStats{SkillName: usage.SkillName}
318: 		}
319: 
320: 		stats.TotalUses++
321: 		stats.Workspaces = append(stats.Workspaces, usage.WorkspaceName)
322: 
323: 		if usage.Success {
324: 			stats.SuccessfulUses++
325: 		} else {
326: 			stats.FailedUses++
327: 		}
328: 
329: 		if stats.TotalUses > 0 {
330: 			stats.SuccessRate = float64(stats.SuccessfulUses) / float64(stats.TotalUses) * 100
331: 		}
332: 
333: 		statsMap[usage.SkillName] = stats
334: 	}
335: 
336: 	return statsMap
337: }
338: 
339: func formatSkillAnalytics(analytics *SkillAnalytics) string {
340: 	var lines []string
341: 
342: 	// Header
343: 	lines = append(lines, "")
344: 	lines = append(lines, strings.Repeat("=", 70))
345: 	lines = append(lines, "📊 SPAWNED AGENT SKILL USAGE (Workspace Markers)")
346: 	lines = append(lines, strings.Repeat("=", 70))
347: 	lines = append(lines, "")
348: 	lines = append(lines, "💡 Context: These are spawned agents created via 'orch spawn {skill}'.")
349: 	lines = append(lines, "   Skills are embedded in SPAWN_CONTEXT.md (not invoked via Skill tool).")
350: 	lines = append(lines, "")
351: 	lines = append(lines, "   Note: Low markers may indicate agents not documenting skills in workspaces,")
352: 	lines = append(lines, "   NOT that skills aren't being used. See --include-transcripts for interactive")
353: 	lines = append(lines, "   Skill tool usage.")
354: 
355: 	// Date range
356: 	if analytics.DateRange != nil {
357: 		lines = append(lines, "")
358: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("📅 Date Range: %s to %s", analytics.DateRange.Start, analytics.DateRange.End))
359: 	}
360: 
361: 	// Overall statistics
362: 	lines = append(lines, "")
363: 	lines = append(lines, "📈 Overall Statistics:")
364: 	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  Total workspaces: %d", analytics.TotalWorkspaces))
365: 	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  Workspaces with skill markers: %d", analytics.WorkspacesWithSkills))
366: 	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  Workspaces without skill markers: %d", analytics.WorkspacesWithoutSkills))
367: 	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  Skill marker rate: %.1f%% (documentation, not usage)", analytics.SkillAdoptionRate))
368: 
369: 	// Skill breakdown
370: 	if len(analytics.Skills) > 0 {
371: 		lines = append(lines, "")
372: 		lines = append(lines, "🎯 Skill Usage Breakdown:")
373: 
374: 		for _, stats := range analytics.Skills {
375: 			successRateStr := "N/A"
376: 			if stats.TotalUses > 0 {
377: 				successRateStr = fmt.Sprintf("%.0f%% success", stats.SuccessRate)
378: 			}
379: 
380: 			usesStr := "use"
381: 			if stats.TotalUses != 1 {
382: 				usesStr = "uses"
383: 			}
384: 			lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  • %s: %d %s (%s)",
385: 				stats.SkillName, stats.TotalUses, usesStr, successRateStr))
386: 
387: 			// Show workspace list if not too many
388: 			if len(stats.Workspaces) <= 5 {
389: 				for _, workspace := range stats.Workspaces {
390: 					lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("      - %s", workspace))
391: 				}
392: 			}
393: 		}
394: 	} else {
395: 		lines = append(lines, "")
396: 		lines = append(lines, "⚠️  No skill usage found in the specified time period.")
397: 	}
398: 
399: 	// Pattern gaps section
400: 	if analytics.WorkspacesWithoutSkills > 0 {
401: 		lines = append(lines, "")
402: 		lines = append(lines, "⚡ Documentation Gap (Not Usage Gap):")
403: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  %d workspace(s) without skill markers in WORKSPACE.md", analytics.WorkspacesWithoutSkills))
404: 		lines = append(lines, "  This indicates missing documentation, not that skills weren't used.")
405: 		lines = append(lines, "  Most spawned agents have skills embedded but don't document in workspace.")
406: 	}
407: 
408: 	lines = append(lines, "")
409: 	lines = append(lines, strings.Repeat("=", 70))
410: 
411: 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
412: }

(End of file - total 412 lines)
</content>

part.text:
**Planning command restructuring**

The new `orch sessions` command must handle untracked sessions exclusively, conflicting with the existing `sessions` command showing history. I'll rename the current `sessions` to `session-history` and implement the new command as the top-level `sessions` to clearly separate tracked and untracked sessions as required.

**Searching for session-related tests**

I'm grepping the repo for "sessions list" to identify any related docs or tests that might influence how the `sessions` commands are implemented.

**Checking API routing structure**

I'm investigating the existing API routing, focusing on the `/api/sessions` and `/api/agents` endpoints, to determine where and how to implement the new `/api/sessions` route.

part.text:
**Inspecting serve.go for session route**

I'm checking serve.go to locate where routes are defined and documented, aiming to add the new /api/sessions endpoint in both help text and routing.

23: // tlsConfigSkipVerify returns a TLS config that skips certificate verification.
24: // Used for connecting to the local server with self-signed certificates.
25: func tlsConfigSkipVerify() *tls.Config {
26: 	return &tls.Config{
27: 		InsecureSkipVerify: true, //nolint:gosec // Self-signed localhost cert
28: 	}

54: var serveCmd = &cobra.Command{
55: 	Use:   "serve",
56: 	Short: "Start the HTTP API server for the beads-ui dashboard",
57: 	Long: `Start an HTTP API server that provides endpoints for the beads-ui dashboard.
58: 
59: This is orchestration infrastructure (persistent monitoring), NOT a project
60: dev server. Use 'orch serve status' to check if the API is running.
61: 
62: Endpoints:
63:   GET /api/agents    - Returns JSON list of active agents from OpenCode/tmux
64:   GET /api/events    - Proxies the OpenCode SSE stream for real-time updates
65:   GET /api/agentlog  - Agent lifecycle events
66:   GET /api/usage     - Claude Max usage stats
67:   GET /api/focus     - Current focus and drift status
68:   GET /api/beads     - Beads stats (ready, blocked, open)
69:   GET /api/beads/ready - List of ready issues for queue visibility
70:   GET /api/beads/graph - Work graph nodes and edges for dependency visualization
71:   GET /api/questions - Questions grouped by status (open, investigating, answered)
72:   GET /api/servers   - Servers status across projects
73:   GET /api/events/services - Service lifecycle events (supports ?follow=true for SSE)
74:   GET /api/daemon    - Daemon status (running, capacity, last poll)
75:   GET /api/gaps      - Gap tracker stats (total, recurring, by-skill)
76:   GET /api/reflect   - Reflect suggestions (synthesis, promote, stale)
77:   GET /api/errors    - Error pattern analysis (recent errors, recurring patterns)
78:   GET /api/hotspot   - Hotspot analysis (fix density, investigation clusters)
79:   GET/PUT /api/config - User configuration settings (~/.orch/config.yaml)
80:   GET /api/changelog - Aggregated changelog (?days=7&project=all)
81:   POST /api/approve  - Approve agent's work (creates beads comment + updates manifest)
82:   GET /health        - Health check
83: 
84: Examples:
85:   orch-go serve              # Start server on port 3348
86:   orch-go serve --port 8080  # Override with explicit port
87:   orch-go serve status       # Check if server is running`,
88: 	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
89: 		return runServe(servePort)
90: 	},
91: }
92: 
93: var serveStatusCmd = &cobra.Command{

96: 	Long: `Check if the orch serve API server is running and accessible.
97: 
98: This command checks if the API server is listening on the expected port
99: and returns its status. The API server is orchestration infrastructure,
100: separate from project dev servers managed by 'orch servers'.
101: 
102: Examples:
103:   orch-go serve status           # Check status on default port
104:   orch-go serve status -p 8080   # Check status on custom port`,
105: 	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
106: 		return runServeStatus(servePort)
107: 	},
108: }

113:

115: 	rootCmd.AddCommand(serveCmd)
116: }

118: // runServeStatus checks if the orch serve API is running on the given port.
119: func runServeStatus(portNum int) error {
120: 	addr := fmt.Sprintf("https://localhost:%d/health", portNum)
121: 
122: 	// Skip TLS verification for self-signed localhost cert
123: 	client := &http.Client{
124: 		Timeout: 2 * time.Second,
125: 		Transport: &http.Transport{
126: 			TLSClientConfig: tlsConfigSkipVerify(),
127: 		},
128: 	}
129: 
130: 	resp, err := client.Get(addr)
131: 	if err != nil {
132: 		fmt.Printf("❌ API server is NOT running on port %d\n", portNum)
133: 		fmt.Println()
134: 		fmt.Println("Start it with: orch serve")
135: 		return nil
136: 	}
137: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
138: 
139: 	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
140: 		fmt.Printf("⚠️  API server on port %d returned status %d\n", portNum, resp.StatusCode)
141: 		return nil
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	// Parse the health response
145: 	var health struct {
146: 		Status string `json:"status"`
147: 	}
148: 	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&health); err != nil {
149: 		fmt.Printf("✅ API server is running on port %d (health check responded)\n", portNum)
150: 		return nil
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	fmt.Printf("✅ API server is running on port %d (HTTP/2 with TLS)\n", portNum)
154: 	fmt.Printf("   Status: %s\n", health.Status)
155: 	fmt.Printf("   URL:    https://localhost:%d\n", portNum)
156: 	fmt.Println()
157: 	fmt.Println("Endpoints:")
158: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/agents    - Active agents")
159: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/events    - SSE event stream")
160: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/agentlog  - Agent lifecycle events")
161: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/usage     - Claude Max usage")
162: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/focus     - Focus and drift status")
163: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/beads     - Beads stats")
164: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/beads/ready - Ready issues list")
165: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/beads/graph - Work graph nodes and edges")
166: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/questions   - Questions grouped by status")
167: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/servers   - Project servers status")
168: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/services  - Service health from overmind monitor")
169: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/events/services - Service lifecycle events (supports ?follow=true for SSE)")
170: 	fmt.Println("  POST /api/issues   - Create new beads issue (for follow-ups)")
171: 	fmt.Println("  POST /api/approve  - Approve agent's work")
172: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/daemon    - Daemon status (running, capacity, last poll)")
173: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/gaps      - Gap tracker stats")
174: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/reflect   - Reflect suggestions")
175: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/errors    - Error pattern analysis")
176: 	fmt.Println("  GET /api/changelog - Aggregated changelog")
177: 	fmt.Println("  GET /health        - Health check")
178: 
179: 	return nil
180: }

182: func runServe(portNum int) error {
183: 	// Set default directory for beads socket discovery
184: 	// This is needed because serve may run from any working directory
185: 	if sourceDir != "" && sourceDir != "unknown" {
186: 		beads.DefaultDir = sourceDir
187: 	}
188: 
189: 	// Resolve bd executable path at startup.
190: 	// This is critical for launchd environments where PATH is minimal.
191: 	// The resolved path is stored in beads.BdPath for use by Fallback* functions.
192: 	if bdPath, err := beads.ResolveBdPath(); err != nil {
193: 		fmt.Printf("Warning: could not resolve bd path (CLI fallback may fail): %v\n", err)
194: 	} else {
195: 		fmt.Printf("Resolved bd path: %s\n", bdPath)
196: 	}
197: 
198: 	// Initialize persistent beads client with auto-reconnect.
199: 	// This avoids per-request connection overhead and handles daemon restarts.
200: 	// Use 5s timeout (not 30s default) to fail fast when daemon dies.
201: 	socketPath, err := beads.FindSocketPath(sourceDir)
202: 	if err == nil {
203: 		beadsClient = beads.NewClient(socketPath,
204: 			beads.WithAutoReconnect(3),
205: 			beads.WithTimeout(5*time.Second),
206: 		)
207: 		if connErr := beadsClient.Connect(); connErr != nil {
208: 			// Non-fatal: handlers will fallback to CLI if client is nil
209: 			fmt.Printf("Warning: beads daemon not available, using CLI fallback: %v\n", connErr)
210: 			beadsClient = nil
211: 		}
212: 	}
213: 
214: 	// Initialize beads cache to prevent CPU spikes from excessive bd spawning.
215: 	// Without caching, each /api/agents request spawns 20+ bd processes for 600+ workspaces.
216: 	globalBeadsCache = newBeadsCache()
217: 
218: 	// Initialize beads stats cache to prevent slow API responses.
219: 	// Without caching, /api/beads spawns bd stats (~1.5s) on every request.
220: 	globalBeadsStatsCache = newBeadsStatsCache()
221: 
222: 	// Start service monitoring daemon (Phase 1 MVP: crash detection + auto-restart)
223: 	// Polls overmind status every 10s, tracks PIDs, emits crash notifications, auto-restarts services
224: 	notifier := notify.Default()
225: 	eventLogger := events.NewDefaultLogger()
226: 	eventAdapter := service.NewEventLoggerAdapter(eventLogger)
227: 	serviceMonitor = service.NewMonitor(sourceDir, notifier, eventAdapter, 10*time.Second, true)
228: 	serviceMonitor.Start()
229: 	fmt.Println("Started service monitor (polling every 10s, auto-restart enabled)")
230: 
231: 	// Initialize LIKELY_DONE cache for attention signals
232: 	globalLikelyDoneCache = attention.NewLikelyDoneCache()
233: 
234: 	// Initialize tree cache
235: 	globalTreeCache = newTreeCache()
236: 
237: 	// Initialize timeline cache
238: 	globalTimelineCache = newTimelineCache()
239: 
240: 	mux := http.NewServeMux()
241: 
242: 	// CORS middleware wrapper
243: 	corsHandler := func(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
244: 		return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
245: 			// Allow requests from SvelteKit dev server and any localhost (http or https)
246: 			origin := r.Header.Get("Origin")
247: 			if origin == "" ||
248: 				strings.HasPrefix(origin, "http://localhost") ||
249: 				strings.HasPrefix(origin, "https://localhost") ||
250: 				strings.HasPrefix(origin, "http://127.0.0.1") ||
251: 				strings.HasPrefix(origin, "https://127.0.0.1") {
252: 				if origin != "" {
253: 					w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", origin)
254: 				} else {
255: 					w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*")
256: 				}
257: 			}
258: 			w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", "GET, POST, PUT, OPTIONS")
259: 			w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", "Content-Type, Accept")
260: 
261: 			// Cache invalidation headers (Phase 4: Dashboard Reliability)
262: 			// Enable dashboard to detect stale data and prompt reload when binary updates
263: 			w.Header().Set("X-Orch-Version", version)
264: 			w.Header().Set("X-Cache-Time", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
265: 
266: 			// Handle preflight
267: 			if r.Method == http.MethodOptions {
268: 				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
269: 				return
270: 			}
271: 
272: 			h(w, r)
273: 		}
274: 	}
275: 
276: 	// GET /api/agents - returns JSON list of agents from OpenCode/tmux
277: 	mux.HandleFunc("/api/agents", corsHandler(handleAgents))
278: 
279: 	// GET /api/events - proxies OpenCode SSE stream
280: 	mux.HandleFunc("/api/events", corsHandler(handleEvents))
281:

464: 	// Start periodic cleanup for stall tracker to prevent memory leaks

478: // handleChangelog returns aggregated changelog data across ecosystem repos.
479: // Query parameters:
480: //   - days: Number of days to include (default: 7)
481: //   - project: Project to filter (default: "all" for all repos)
482: func handleChangelog(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
483: 	if r.Method != http.MethodGet {
484: 		http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
485: 		return
486: 	}
487: 
488: 	// Parse query parameters
489: 	daysStr := r.URL.Query().Get("days")
490: 	project := r.URL.Query().Get("project")
491: 
492: 	// Default values
493: 	days := 7
494: 	if daysStr != "" {
495: 		if d, err := strconv.Atoi(daysStr); err == nil && d > 0 {
496: 			days = d
497: 		}
498: 	}
499: 	if project == "" {
500: 		project = "all"
501: 	}
502: 
503: 	// Get changelog data using the reusable function from changelog.go
504: 	result, err := GetChangelog(days, project)
505: 	if err != nil {
506: 		http.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf("Failed to get changelog: %v", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)
507: 		return
508: 	}
509: 
510: 	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
511: 	if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result); err != nil {
512: 		http.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf("Failed to encode changelog: %v", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)
513: 		return
514: 	}
515: }

17: // DefaultServerURL is the default OpenCode server URL.
18: // Uses 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost to avoid IPv6 resolution issues on macOS.
19: // On macOS, localhost can resolve to IPv6 ::1 while the server binds to IPv4,
20: // causing "connection refused" errors.
21: const DefaultServerURL = "http://127.0.0.1:4096"

23: // DefaultHTTPTimeout is the default timeout for HTTP requests to the OpenCode API.
24: // This prevents hangs when OpenCode is in a bad state (e.g., redirect loop).
25: const DefaultHTTPTimeout = 10 * time.Second
26: 
27: // LargeScannerBufferSize is the buffer size for scanning JSON events from opencode output.
28: // OpenCode JSON events can be very large (especially tool outputs with file contents),
29: // so we use 1MB instead of the default 64KB (bufio.MaxScanTokenSize) to prevent ErrTooLong.
30: const LargeScannerBufferSize = 1024 * 1024 // 1MB

32: // Client handles OpenCode CLI interactions.

43: 			Timeout: DefaultHTTPTimeout,
44: 			// Limit redirects to prevent redirect loops from hanging
45: 			CheckRedirect: func(req *http.Request, via []*http.Request) error {

50: 			},

78: // ParseEvent parses a JSON event from opencode output.
79: func ParseEvent(line string) (Event, error) {
80: 	var event Event

83: 	}

87: // ExtractSessionID extracts session ID from opencode events.
88: // OpenCode includes sessionID at the top level of each event.
89: func ExtractSessionID(events []string) (string, error) {
90: 	for _, line := range events {
91: 		event, err := ParseEvent(line)
92: 		if err != nil {

94: 		}
95: 		if event.SessionID != "" {
96: 			return event.SessionID, nil
97: 		}
98: 	}
99: 	return "", ErrNoSessionID
100: }

102: // ExtractSessionIDFromReader reads from a reader until it finds a session ID.
103: // Returns as soon as a session ID is found, leaving remaining data unread.
104: // This is useful for headless spawns where we need the session ID quickly
105: // but don't want to block waiting for the process to complete.
106: func ExtractSessionIDFromReader(r io.Reader) (string, error) {
107: 	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(r)
108: 	// Use large buffer to handle OpenCode's potentially large JSON events
109: 	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, LargeScannerBufferSize), LargeScannerBufferSize)
110: 	for scanner.Scan() {
111: 		line := scanner.Text()
112: 		if line == "" {

130: // ProcessOutput processes the output from opencode command.
131: func ProcessOutput(r io.Reader) (*Result, error) {

133: 	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(r)
134: 	// Use large buffer to handle OpenCode's potentially large JSON events
135: 	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, LargeScannerBufferSize), LargeScannerBufferSize)

167: 	result := &Result{}

200:

233: // SendMessageAsync sends a message to an existing session asynchronously.
234: // The model parameter is optional - if empty, OpenCode will use the default model.
235: // Model should be in "provider/modelID" format (e.g., "google/gemini-2.5-flash").
236: func (c *Client) SendMessageAsync(sessionID, content, model string) error {
237: 	payload := map[string]any{
238: 		"parts": []map[string]string{{"type": "text", "text": content}},
239: 		"agent": "build",
240: 	}
241: 	// Include model if provided (per-message model in OpenCode)
242: 	// OpenCode expects model as an object with providerID and modelID fields
243: 	if model != "" {
244: 		modelObj := parseModelSpec(model)
245: 		if modelObj != nil {
246: 			payload["model"] = modelObj
247: 		}
248: 	}
249: 	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
250: 	if err != nil {
251: 		return err
252: 	}
253: 
254: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID+"/prompt_async", bytes.NewReader(body))
255: 	if err != nil {
256: 		return err
257: 	}
258: 	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
259: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
260: 	if err != nil {
261: 		return err
262: 	}
263: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
264: 
265: 	if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
266: 		respBody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
267: 		return fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code: %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(respBody))
268: 	}
269: 	return nil
270: }

272: // parseModelSpec parses a model string in "provider/modelID" format
273: // and returns a map suitable for the OpenCode API.
274: // Returns nil if the model string is empty or invalid.
275: func parseModelSpec(model string) map[string]string {
276: 	if model == "" {
277: 		return nil
278: 	}
279: 	// Expected format: "provider/modelID" (e.g., "google/gemini-2.5-flash")
280: 	idx := strings.Index(model, "/")
281: 	if idx <= 0 || idx >= len(model)-1 {
282: 		// Invalid format - no slash or empty provider/model
283: 		return nil
284: 	}
285: 	return map[string]string{
286: 		"providerID": model[:idx],
287: 		"modelID":    model[idx+1:],
288: 	}
289: }

291: // ListSessions fetches all sessions from the OpenCode API.
292: // If directory is provided, it passes it via x-opencode-directory header.
293: func (c *Client) ListSessions(directory string) ([]Session, error) {
294: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session", nil)
295: 	if err != nil {
296: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)

298: 
299: 	if directory != "" {
300: 		req.Header.Set("x-opencode-directory", directory)

302: 
303: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)

306: 	}
307: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
308: 
309: 	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {

312: 
313: 	var sessions []Session

345: // GetSessionInDirectory fetches a session and scopes it to the provided directory.
346: // This is required when the server uses x-opencode-directory to select project context.
347: func (c *Client) GetSessionInDirectory(sessionID, directory string) (*Session, error) {
348: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID, nil)
349: 	if err != nil {
350: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
351: 	}
352: 	if directory != "" {
353: 		req.Header.Set("x-opencode-directory", directory)

357: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch session: %w", err)

373: // VerifySessionAfterPrompt checks that a session exists in the given directory
374: // after sending a prompt and listens briefly for session.error events.
375: // Returns an error if the session cannot be fetched or reports an error event.
376: func (c *Client) VerifySessionAfterPrompt(sessionID, directory string, timeout time.Duration) error {
377: 	if _, err := c.GetSessionInDirectory(sessionID, directory); err != nil {
378: 		return fmt.Errorf("session not found after prompt: %w", err)
379: 	}
380: 
381: 	errMsg, err := c.WaitForSessionError(sessionID, timeout)
382: 	if err != nil {
383: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to monitor session for errors: %w", err)
384: 	}
385: 	if errMsg != "" {
386: 		return fmt.Errorf("session reported error: %s", errMsg)
387: 	}
388: 
389: 	return nil
390: }

392: // SessionExists checks if a session exists in OpenCode (in-memory).
393: // Returns true if the session is accessible via the API, false otherwise.
394: // NOTE: This returns true for any persisted session, not just actively running ones.
395: // For liveness checks, use IsSessionActive() instead.
396: func (c *Client) SessionExists(sessionID string) bool {
397: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID, nil)
398: 	if err != nil {
399: 		return false
400: 	}
401: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
402: 	if err != nil {
403: 		return false
404: 	}
405: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
406: 
407: 	// Session exists if we get 200 OK
408: 	return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
409: }
410: 
411: // WaitForSessionError listens for session.error events for a limited time.
412: // Returns the error message if detected, empty string if none, or an error on connection failure.
413: func (c *Client) WaitForSessionError(sessionID string, timeout time.Duration) (string, error) {
414: 	if timeout <= 0 {
415: 		return "", nil
416: 	}
417: 
418: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)
419: 	defer cancel()
420: 
421: 	sseClient := NewSSEClient(c.ServerURL + "/event")
422: 	events := make(chan SSEEvent, 10)
423: 	errCh := make(chan error, 1)
424: 
425: 	go func() {
426: 		errCh <- sseClient.ConnectWithContext(ctx, events)
427: 		close(events)
428: 	}()
429: 
430: 	for {
431: 		select {
432: 		case <-ctx.Done():
433: 			return "", nil
434: 		case event, ok := <-events:
435: 			if !ok {
436: 				select {
437: 				case err := <-errCh:
438: 					if err != nil && ctx.Err() == nil {
439: 						return "", err
440: 					}
441: 				default:
442: 				}
443: 				return "", nil
444: 			}
445: 			if event.Event != "session.error" && event.Event != "" {
446: 				continue
447: 			}
448: 			sid, msg := ParseSessionError(event.Data)
449: 			if sid == sessionID && msg != "" {
450: 				return msg, nil
451: 			}
452: 		case err := <-errCh:
453: 			if err != nil && ctx.Err() == nil {
454: 				return "", err
455: 			}
456: 			return "", nil
457: 		}
458: 	}
459: }
460: 
461: // IsSessionActive checks if a session is actively running (updated within maxIdleTime).
462: // This is more reliable than SessionExists() for liveness detection because OpenCode
463: // persists sessions to disk, so SessionExists() returns true for historical sessions.
464: // maxIdleTime is the maximum time since last update to consider a session "active".
465: func (c *Client) IsSessionActive(sessionID string, maxIdleTime time.Duration) bool {
466: 	session, err := c.GetSession(sessionID)
467: 	if err != nil {
468: 		return false
469: 	}
470: 
471: 	updatedAt := time.Unix(session.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
472: 	idleTime := time.Since(updatedAt)
473: 	return idleTime <= maxIdleTime
474: }
475: 
476: // IsSessionProcessing checks if a session is actively processing (has a pending assistant response).
477: // This is the most reliable signal for detecting truly active agents because it checks
478: // whether the last assistant message has finished (finish != "" and completed != 0).
479: // Returns true if the session is currently generating a response, false if idle.
480: func (c *Client) IsSessionProcessing(sessionID string) bool {
481: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
482: 	if err != nil || len(messages) == 0 {
483: 		return false
484: 	}
485: 
486: 	// Find the last message
487: 	lastMsg := messages[len(messages)-1]
488: 
489: 	// Session is processing if:
490: 	// 1. Last message is from assistant AND
491: 	// 2. It hasn't finished yet (finish is empty and completed is 0)
492: 	if lastMsg.Info.Role == "assistant" {
493: 		return lastMsg.Info.Finish == "" && lastMsg.Info.Time.Completed == 0
494: 	}
495: 
496: 	// If last message is from user, check if there's an assistant response being generated
497: 	// This happens when user sends a message and assistant hasn't started responding yet
498: 	// In this case, we consider the session as processing (waiting for response)
499: 	if lastMsg.Info.Role == "user" {
500: 		// If the user message was sent recently (within last 30 seconds), consider it processing
501: 		createdAt := time.Unix(lastMsg.Info.Time.Created/1000, 0)
502: 		return time.Since(createdAt) < 30*time.Second
503: 	}
504: 
505: 	return false
506: }
507: 
508: // GetLastMessage returns the last message in a session, or nil if the session has no messages.
509: func (c *Client) GetLastMessage(sessionID string) (*Message, error) {
510: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
511: 	if err != nil {
512: 		return nil, err
513: 	}
514: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
515: 		return nil, nil
516: 	}
517: 	return &messages[len(messages)-1], nil
518: }
519: 
520: // LastActivity represents the most recent activity from a session.
521: // This is used to populate agent activity on initial dashboard load.
522: type LastActivity struct {
523: 	Text      string // Activity description (truncated for display)
524: 	Timestamp int64  // Unix timestamp in milliseconds
525: }
526: 
527: // GetLastActivity extracts the last meaningful activity from session messages.
528: // It looks for the most recent assistant message and extracts a summary of what
529: // the agent is doing (tool use, text generation, etc.).
530: // Returns nil if no activity can be extracted.
531: func (c *Client) GetLastActivity(sessionID string) (*LastActivity, error) {
532: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
533: 	if err != nil {
534: 		return nil, err
535: 	}
536: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
537: 		return nil, nil
538: 	}
539: 
540: 	// Find the last assistant message (most relevant for activity)
541: 	var lastAssistantMsg *Message
542: 	for i := len(messages) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
543: 		if messages[i].Info.Role == "assistant" {
544: 			lastAssistantMsg = &messages[i]
545: 			break
546: 		}
547: 	}
548: 
549: 	if lastAssistantMsg == nil {
550: 		return nil, nil
551: 	}
552: 
553: 	// Extract activity from message parts
554: 	// Priority: tool invocation > text > reasoning
555: 	var activityText string
556: 	for _, part := range lastAssistantMsg.Parts {
557: 		switch part.Type {
558: 		case "tool-invocation", "tool":
559: 			// Tool use is the most informative activity
560: 			activityText = "Using tool: " + extractToolName(part.Text)
561: 			break
562: 		case "text":
563: 			if part.Text != "" && activityText == "" {
564: 				// Truncate long text
565: 				activityText = truncateText(part.Text, 80)
566: 			}
567: 		case "reasoning":
568: 			if activityText == "" {
569: 				activityText = "Thinking..."
570: 			}
571: 		}
572: 	}
573: 
574: 	if activityText == "" {
575: 		return nil, nil
576: 	}
577: 
578: 	// Use message completion time if available, otherwise created time
579: 	timestamp := lastAssistantMsg.Info.Time.Completed
580: 	if timestamp == 0 {
581: 		timestamp = lastAssistantMsg.Info.Time.Created
582: 	}
583: 
584: 	return &LastActivity{
585: 		Text:      activityText,
586: 		Timestamp: timestamp,
587: 	}, nil
588: }
589: 
590: // extractToolName tries to extract a tool name from tool invocation text.
591: // Tool text can be JSON or plain text depending on OpenCode version.
592: func extractToolName(text string) string {
593: 	// Try to extract tool name - the text might be structured or plain
594: 	if text == "" {
595: 		return "unknown"
596: 	}
597: 	// Truncate for display
598: 	if len(text) > 50 {
599: 		return text[:50] + "..."
600: 	}
601: 	return text
602: }
603: 
604: // truncateText truncates text to maxLen characters with ellipsis.
605: func truncateText(text string, maxLen int) string {
606: 	if len(text) <= maxLen {
607: 		return text
608: 	}
609: 	// Find last space before maxLen to avoid cutting words
610: 	for i := maxLen - 3; i > 0; i-- {
611: 		if text[i] == ' ' {
612: 			return text[:i] + "..."
613: 		}
614: 	}
615: 	return text[:maxLen-3] + "..."
616: }

618: // CreateSessionRequest represents the request body for creating a new session.
619: type CreateSessionRequest struct {
620: 	Title     string            `json:"title,omitempty"`
621: 	Directory string            `json:"directory,omitempty"`
622: 	Model     string            `json:"model,omitempty"`
623: 	Metadata  map[string]string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
624: 	TimeTTL   int               `json:"time_ttl,omitempty"` // Session TTL in seconds (0 = no expiration)
625: }
626: 
627: // CreateSessionResponse represents the response from creating a new session.
628: type CreateSessionResponse struct {
629: 	ID        string `json:"id"`
630: 	Title     string `json:"title,omitempty"`
631: 	Directory string `json:"directory,omitempty"`
632: }
633: 
634: // CreateSession creates a new OpenCode session via HTTP API.
635: // This is used for headless spawns (no tmux window).
636: // timeTTL is the session expiration time in seconds (0 = no expiration).
637: func (c *Client) CreateSession(title, directory, model string, metadata map[string]string, timeTTL int) (*CreateSessionResponse, error) {
638: 	payload := CreateSessionRequest{
639: 		Title:     title,
640: 		Directory: directory,
641: 		Model:     model,
642: 		Metadata:  metadata,
643: 		TimeTTL:   timeTTL,
644: 	}
645: 	body, err := json.Marshal(payload)
646: 	if err != nil {
647: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal request: %w", err)
648: 	}
649: 
650: 	// Create request to set custom headers
651: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", c.ServerURL+"/session", bytes.NewReader(body))
652: 	if err != nil {
653: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
654: 	}
655: 	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
656: 
657: 	// OpenCode expects directory via x-opencode-directory header
658: 	if directory != "" {
659: 		req.Header.Set("x-opencode-directory", directory)
660: 	}
661: 
662: 	// Set ORCH_WORKER=1 header to signal this is an orch-managed worker session
663: 	// This allows the session-context plugin to skip loading orchestrator skill
664: 	req.Header.Set("x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER", "1")
665: 
666: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
667: 	if err != nil {
668: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create session: %w", err)
669: 	}
670: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
671: 
672: 	if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
673: 		respBody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
674: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(respBody))
675: 	}
676: 
677: 	var result CreateSessionResponse
678: 	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&result); err != nil {
679: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to decode response: %w", err)
680: 	}
681: 
682: 	return &result, nil
683: }

741: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID+"/prompt_async", bytes.NewReader(body))

748: 	}
749: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
750: 	if err != nil {

762: // WaitForSessionIdle polls GET /session/status and blocks until the specified session
763: // transitions from busy to idle. Used by inline spawn mode to maintain blocking
764: // behavior while using the HTTP API for session creation.
765: //
766: // Replaced SSE-based implementation with polling for simplicity. Polls every 500ms.
767: func (c *Client) WaitForSessionIdle(sessionID string) error {
768: 	const pollInterval = 500 * time.Millisecond
769: 	const maxWait = 10 * time.Minute // Safety timeout
770: 
771: 	deadline := time.Now().Add(maxWait)
772: 	var sessionWasBusy bool
773: 
774: 	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
775: 		status, err := c.GetSessionStatusByID(sessionID)
776: 		if err != nil {
777: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to get session status: %w", err)
778: 		}
779: 
780: 		// Track if session was ever busy
781: 		if status.IsBusy() || status.IsRetrying() {
782: 			sessionWasBusy = true
783: 		}
784: 
785: 		// Completion: session was busy and is now idle
786: 		if sessionWasBusy && status.IsIdle() {
787: 			return nil
788: 		}
789: 
790: 		// Poll again after interval
791: 		time.Sleep(pollInterval)
792: 	}
793: 
794: 	return fmt.Errorf("timeout waiting for session %s to complete (max %v)", sessionID, maxWait)
795: }

797: // GetMessages fetches all messages for a session from the OpenCode API.

808: 
809: 	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {

811: 	}
812: 
813: 	var messages []Message
814: 	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&messages); err != nil {
815: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to decode messages: %w", err)
816: 	}

818: 	return messages, nil

821: // FindRecentSession finds the most recent session for a given project directory.
822: // It matches by directory and creation time only (within 30 seconds).
823: // Title matching is not used because OpenCode session titles are set to the first
824: // prompt text, not the workspace name, making title matching unreliable.
825: func (c *Client) FindRecentSession(projectDir string) (string, error) {
826: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session", nil)
827: 	if err != nil {
828: 		return "", err
829: 	}
830: 	req.Header.Set("x-opencode-directory", projectDir)
831: 
832: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
833: 	if err != nil {
834: 		return "", err
835: 	}
836: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
837: 
838: 	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
839: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code: %d", resp.StatusCode)
840: 	}
841: 
842: 	var sessions []Session
843: 	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&sessions); err != nil {
844: 		return "", err
845: 	}
846: 
847: 	// Find the most recent session for this directory (within 30 seconds)
848: 	var mostRecent *Session
849: 	now := time.Now().UnixMilli()
850: 	for i := range sessions {
851: 		s := &sessions[i]
852: 		if s.Directory != projectDir {
853: 			continue
854: 		}
855: 		// Only match sessions created in the last 30 seconds
856: 		if now-s.Time.Created > 30*1000 {
857: 			continue
858: 		}
859: 		if mostRecent == nil || s.Time.Created > mostRecent.Time.Created {
860: 			mostRecent = s
861: 		}
862: 	}
863: 
864: 	if mostRecent == nil {
865: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("no sessions found for directory: %s (within last 30s)", projectDir)
866: 	}
867: 
868: 	return mostRecent.ID, nil
869: }
870: 
871: // FindRecentSessionWithRetry retries finding a recent session with exponential backoff.
872: // This handles the race condition where OpenCode TUI starts before registering with the API.
873: // Returns the session ID if found, or empty string with error if not found after retries.
874: func (c *Client) FindRecentSessionWithRetry(projectDir string, maxAttempts int, initialDelay time.Duration) (string, error) {
875: 	delay := initialDelay
876: 	var lastErr error
877: 
878: 	for attempt := 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++ {
879: 		sessionID, err := c.FindRecentSession(projectDir)
880: 		if err == nil {
881: 			return sessionID, nil
882: 		}
883: 		lastErr = err
884: 
885: 		// Don't sleep after the last attempt
886: 		if attempt < maxAttempts {
887: 			time.Sleep(delay)
888: 			delay = delay * 2 // Exponential backoff
889: 		}
890: 	}
891: 
892: 	// Return empty string (not an error) since tmux window_id is sufficient for monitoring
893: 	return "", lastErr
894: }
895: 
896: // ExtractRecentText extracts the most recent text content from messages.
897: // It returns up to `lines` worth of text from the most recent messages.
898: // The text is extracted from message parts of type "text".
899: func ExtractRecentText(messages []Message, lines int) []string {
900: 	var result []string
901: 
902: 	// Process messages in reverse order (most recent first)
903: 	for i := len(messages) - 1; i >= 0 && len(result) < lines; i-- {
904: 		msg := messages[i]
905: 
906: 		// Extract text from parts
907: 		for j := len(msg.Parts) - 1; j >= 0 && len(result) < lines; j-- {
908: 			part := msg.Parts[j]
909: 			if part.Type == "text" && part.Text != "" {
910: 				// Split text into lines and add in reverse
911: 				textLines := strings.Split(part.Text, "\n")
912: 				for k := len(textLines) - 1; k >= 0 && len(result) < lines; k-- {
913: 					line := textLines[k]
914: 					if line != "" || len(result) > 0 { // Skip leading empty lines
915: 						result = append([]string{line}, result...)
916: 					}
917: 				}
918: 			}
919: 		}
920: 	}
921: 
922: 	// Trim to requested line count
923: 	if len(result) > lines {
924: 		result = result[len(result)-lines:]
925: 	}
926: 
927: 	return result
928: }

964: // DeleteSession deletes an OpenCode session by ID.
965: // Returns nil on success, error on failure.
966: func (c *Client) DeleteSession(sessionID string) error {
967: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("DELETE", c.ServerURL+"/session/"+sessionID, nil)
968: 	if err != nil {
969: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
970: 	}
971: 
972: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
973: 	if err != nil {
974: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to delete session: %w", err)
975: 	}
976: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
977: 
978: 	// Accept 200 OK or 204 No Content as success

987: // SendMessageWithStreaming sends a message to a session and streams the response.
988: // It sends the message via the async API, then connects to SSE to stream text events
989: // until the session becomes idle. Text content is written to the provided writer.
990: func (c *Client) SendMessageWithStreaming(sessionID, content string, streamTo io.Writer) error {
991: 	// Send the message via async API first (no model specified for Q&A)
992: 	if err := c.SendMessageAsync(sessionID, content, ""); err != nil {
993: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to send message: %w", err)
994: 	}
995: 
996: 	// Connect to SSE and stream the response
997: 	// Use a client without timeout for SSE - it's a long-running stream
998: 	sseClient := &http.Client{
999: 		// No timeout - SSE is meant to be long-running
1000: 		// But still limit redirects
1001: 		CheckRedirect: func(req *http.Request, via []*http.Request) error {
1002: 			if len(via) >= 10 {
1003: 				return fmt.Errorf("too many redirects (max 10)")
1004: 			}
1005: 			return nil
1006: 		},
1007: 	}
1008: 	sseURL := c.ServerURL + "/event"
1009: 	resp, err := sseClient.Get(sseURL)
1010: 	if err != nil {
1011: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to connect to SSE: %w", err)
1012: 	}
1013: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
1014: 
1015: 	reader := bufio.NewReader(resp.Body)
1016: 	var eventBuffer strings.Builder
1017: 	var sessionWasBusy bool
1018: 	var messageIDSeen = make(map[string]bool) // Track message IDs to avoid duplicates
1019: 
1020: 	for {
1021: 		line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
1022: 		if err != nil {
1023: 			if err == io.EOF {
1024: 				return nil
1025: 			}
1026: 			return err
1027: 		}
1028: 
1029: 		eventBuffer.WriteString(line)
1030: 
1031: 		// Empty line signals end of event
1032: 		if line == "\n" && eventBuffer.Len() > 1 {
1033: 			raw := eventBuffer.String()
1034: 			eventType, data := ParseSSEEvent(raw)
1035: 			eventBuffer.Reset()
1036: 
1037: 			if data == "" {
1038: 				continue
1039: 			}
1040: 
1041: 			// Parse the JSON data
1042: 			var eventData map[string]interface{}
1043: 			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(data), &eventData); err != nil {
1044: 				continue
1045: 			}
1046: 
1047: 			// Check if this event is for our session
1048: 			eventSessionID := ""
1049: 			if props, ok := eventData["properties"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1050: 				if sid, ok := props["sessionID"].(string); ok {
1051: 					eventSessionID = sid
1052: 				}
1053: 			}
1054: 			// Also check top-level sessionID
1055: 			if sid, ok := eventData["sessionID"].(string); ok && eventSessionID == "" {
1056: 				eventSessionID = sid
1057: 			}
1058: 
1059: 			// Skip events from other sessions
1060: 			if eventSessionID != "" && eventSessionID != sessionID {
1061: 				continue
1062: 			}
1063: 
1064: 			// Handle session error events
1065: 			if eventType == "session.error" {
1066: 				if props, ok := eventData["properties"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1067: 					// Check if this error is for our session
1068: 					if sid, ok := props["sessionID"].(string); ok && sid == sessionID {
1069: 						// Extract error details
1070: 						if errorObj, ok := props["error"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1071: 							if msg, ok := errorObj["message"].(string); ok {
1072: 								return fmt.Errorf("session error: %s", msg)
1073: 							}
1074: 						}
1075: 						return fmt.Errorf("session error occurred")
1076: 					}
1077: 				}
1078: 				// Also check top-level sessionID for older format
1079: 				if sid, ok := eventData["sessionID"].(string); ok && sid == sessionID {
1080: 					if errorObj, ok := eventData["error"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1081: 						if msg, ok := errorObj["message"].(string); ok {
1082: 							return fmt.Errorf("session error: %s", msg)
1083: 						}
1084: 					}
1085: 					return fmt.Errorf("session error occurred")
1086: 				}
1087: 				continue
1088: 			}
1089: 
1090: 			// Handle session status events
1091: 			if eventType == "session.status" {
1092: 				status, sid := ParseSessionStatus(data)
1093: 				if sid == sessionID {
1094: 					if status == "busy" || status == "running" {
1095: 						sessionWasBusy = true
1096: 					}
1097: 					// Completion: session was busy and is now idle
1098: 					if sessionWasBusy && status == "idle" {
1099: 						return nil
1100: 					}
1101: 				}
1102: 				continue
1103: 			}
1104: 
1105: 			// Handle message.part events (text streaming)
1106: 			if eventType == "message.part" {
1107: 				if props, ok := eventData["properties"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1108: 					// Check this is for our session
1109: 					if sid, ok := props["sessionID"].(string); ok && sid != sessionID {
1110: 						continue
1111: 					}
1112: 
1113: 					// Get message ID to track what we've seen
1114: 					messageID := ""
1115: 					if mid, ok := props["messageID"].(string); ok {
1116: 						messageID = mid
1117: 					}
1118: 
1119: 					// Get the part data
1120: 					if part, ok := props["part"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
1121: 						if partType, ok := part["type"].(string); ok && partType == "text" {
1122: 							if text, ok := part["text"].(string); ok && text != "" {
1123: 								// Write text to output
1124: 								streamTo.Write([]byte(text))
1125: 							}
1126: 						}
1127: 					}
1128: 
1129: 					// Track message ID if provided
1130: 					if messageID != "" {
1131: 						messageIDSeen[messageID] = true
1132: 					}
1133: 				}
1134: 			}
1135: 		}
1136: 	}
1137: }
