1139: // TokenStats represents aggregated token usage for a session.
1140: type TokenStats struct {
1141: 	InputTokens     int `json:"input_tokens"`
1142: 	OutputTokens    int `json:"output_tokens"`
1143: 	ReasoningTokens int `json:"reasoning_tokens,omitempty"`
1144: 	CacheReadTokens int `json:"cache_read_tokens,omitempty"`
1145: 	TotalTokens     int `json:"total_tokens"` // input + output + reasoning
1146: }
1147: 
1148: // AggregateTokens calculates total token usage from a slice of messages.
1149: // It sums up input, output, reasoning, and cache tokens across all messages.
1150: func AggregateTokens(messages []Message) TokenStats {
1151: 	var stats TokenStats
1152: 	for _, msg := range messages {
1153: 		if msg.Info.Tokens == nil {
1154: 			continue
1155: 		}
1156: 		stats.InputTokens += msg.Info.Tokens.Input
1157: 		stats.OutputTokens += msg.Info.Tokens.Output
1158: 		stats.ReasoningTokens += msg.Info.Tokens.Reasoning
1159: 		if msg.Info.Tokens.Cache != nil {
1160: 			stats.CacheReadTokens += msg.Info.Tokens.Cache.Read
1161: 		}
1162: 	}
1163: 	stats.TotalTokens = stats.InputTokens + stats.OutputTokens + stats.ReasoningTokens
1164: 	return stats
1165: }
1166: 
1167: // GetSessionTokens fetches messages for a session and returns aggregated token stats.
1168: // Returns nil if session doesn't exist or has no messages.
1169: func (c *Client) GetSessionTokens(sessionID string) (*TokenStats, error) {
1170: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
1171: 	if err != nil {
1172: 		return nil, err
1173: 	}
1174: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
1175: 		return nil, nil
1176: 	}
1177: 	stats := AggregateTokens(messages)
1178: 	return &stats, nil
1179: }
1180: 
1181: // GetAllSessionStatus fetches the status of all sessions from GET /session/status endpoint.
1182: // Returns a map of sessionID -> SessionStatusInfo.
1183: // Sessions not in the map are considered idle (status is in-memory only in OpenCode).
1184: func (c *Client) GetAllSessionStatus() (map[string]SessionStatusInfo, error) {
1185: 	req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", c.ServerURL+"/session/status", nil)
1186: 	if err != nil {
1187: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request: %w", err)
1188: 	}
1189: 
1190: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
1191: 	if err != nil {
1192: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch session status: %w", err)
1193: 	}
1194: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
1195: 
1196: 	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
1197: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected status code: %d", resp.StatusCode)
1198: 	}
1199: 
1200: 	var result map[string]SessionStatusInfo
1201: 	if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&result); err != nil {
1202: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to decode session status: %w", err)
1203: 	}
1204: 
1205: 	return result, nil
1206: }
1207: 
1208: // GetSessionStatusByID fetches the status of a specific session.
1209: // Returns nil if session is idle (status deleted from in-memory map in OpenCode).
1210: // Use SessionExists() to distinguish between "session doesn't exist" vs "session is idle".
1211: func (c *Client) GetSessionStatusByID(sessionID string) (*SessionStatusInfo, error) {
1212: 	allStatus, err := c.GetAllSessionStatus()
1213: 	if err != nil {
1214: 		return nil, err
1215: 	}
1216: 
1217: 	status, exists := allStatus[sessionID]
1218: 	if !exists {
1219: 		// Session not in status map = idle (or doesn't exist)
1220: 		return &SessionStatusInfo{Type: "idle"}, nil
1221: 	}
1222: 
1223: 	return &status, nil
1224: }
1225: 
1226: // ExportSessionTranscript fetches all messages for a session and formats them as markdown.
1227: // This is useful for preserving conversation history before deleting a session.
1228: // Returns the markdown transcript and any error encountered.
1229: func (c *Client) ExportSessionTranscript(sessionID string) (string, error) {
1230: 	// Get session info
1231: 	session, err := c.GetSession(sessionID)
1232: 	if err != nil {
1233: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get session: %w", err)
1234: 	}
1235: 
1236: 	// Get all messages
1237: 	messages, err := c.GetMessages(sessionID)
1238: 	if err != nil {
1239: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get messages: %w", err)
1240: 	}
1241: 
1242: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
1243: 		return "", nil // No messages to export
1244: 	}
1245: 
1246: 	return FormatMessagesAsTranscript(session, messages), nil
1247: }
1248: 
1249: // FormatMessagesAsTranscript converts session info and messages to a markdown transcript.
1250: func FormatMessagesAsTranscript(session *Session, messages []Message) string {
1251: 	var lines []string
1252: 
1253: 	// Header
1254: 	lines = append(lines, "# Session Transcript", "")
1255: 
1256: 	// Session metadata
1257: 	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Title:** %s", session.Title))
1258: 	lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Session ID:** `%s`", session.ID))
1259: 	if session.Directory != "" {
1260: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Directory:** `%s`", session.Directory))
1261: 	}
1262: 	if session.Time.Created > 0 {
1263: 		t := time.Unix(session.Time.Created/1000, 0)
1264: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Started:** %s", t.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")))
1265: 	}
1266: 	if session.Time.Updated > 0 {
1267: 		t := time.Unix(session.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
1268: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Updated:** %s", t.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")))
1269: 	}
1270: 
1271: 	// Summary stats
1272: 	if session.Summary.Additions > 0 || session.Summary.Deletions > 0 || session.Summary.Files > 0 {
1273: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("**Changes:** +%d/-%d in %d files",
1274: 			session.Summary.Additions, session.Summary.Deletions, session.Summary.Files))
1275: 	}
1276: 
1277: 	lines = append(lines, "", "---", "")
1278: 
1279: 	// Format messages
1280: 	for _, msg := range messages {
1281: 		formatted := formatMessageToMarkdown(&msg)
1282: 		if formatted != "" {
1283: 			lines = append(lines, formatted)
1284: 		}
1285: 	}
1286: 
1287: 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
1288: }
1289: 
1290: // formatMessageToMarkdown formats a single message to markdown.
1291: func formatMessageToMarkdown(msg *Message) string {
1292: 	// Collect text parts and tool parts
1293: 	var textParts []string
1294: 	var toolParts []MessagePart
1295: 
1296: 	for _, part := range msg.Parts {
1297: 		switch part.Type {
1298: 		case "text":
1299: 			text := strings.TrimSpace(part.Text)
1300: 			if text != "" {
1301: 				textParts = append(textParts, text)
1302: 			}
1303: 		case "tool", "tool-invocation":
1304: 			toolParts = append(toolParts, part)
1305: 		}
1306: 	}
1307: 
1308: 	// Skip message if no content
1309: 	if len(textParts) == 0 && len(toolParts) == 0 {
1310: 		return ""
1311: 	}
1312: 
1313: 	var lines []string
1314: 
1315: 	// Header with role and timestamp
1316: 	var timestamp string
1317: 	if msg.Info.Time.Created > 0 {
1318: 		t := time.Unix(msg.Info.Time.Created/1000, 0)
1319: 		timestamp = t.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")
1320: 	}
1321: 
1322: 	switch msg.Info.Role {
1323: 	case "user":
1324: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("## User (%s)", timestamp))
1325: 	case "assistant":
1326: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("## Assistant (%s)", timestamp))
1327: 		// Add token/cost info
1328: 		if msg.Info.Tokens != nil {
1329: 			var tokenInfo []string
1330: 			if msg.Info.Tokens.Input > 0 {
1331: 				tokenInfo = append(tokenInfo, fmt.Sprintf("in:%d", msg.Info.Tokens.Input))
1332: 			}
1333: 			if msg.Info.Tokens.Output > 0 {
1334: 				tokenInfo = append(tokenInfo, fmt.Sprintf("out:%d", msg.Info.Tokens.Output))
1335: 			}
1336: 			if msg.Info.Tokens.Cache != nil && msg.Info.Tokens.Cache.Read > 0 {
1337: 				tokenInfo = append(tokenInfo, fmt.Sprintf("cached:%d", msg.Info.Tokens.Cache.Read))
1338: 			}
1339: 			if msg.Info.Cost > 0 {
1340: 				tokenInfo = append(tokenInfo, fmt.Sprintf("$%.4f", msg.Info.Cost))
1341: 			}
1342: 			if len(tokenInfo) > 0 {
1343: 				lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("*Tokens: %s*", strings.Join(tokenInfo, ", ")))
1344: 			}
1345: 		}
1346: 	default:
1347: 		role := msg.Info.Role
1348: 		if role != "" {
1349: 			role = strings.ToUpper(role[:1]) + role[1:]
1350: 		}
1351: 		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("## %s (%s)", role, timestamp))
1352: 	}
1353: 
1354: 	lines = append(lines, "")
1355: 
1356: 	// Add text content
1357: 	for _, text := range textParts {
1358: 		lines = append(lines, text, "")
1359: 	}
1360: 
1361: 	// Add tool summaries
1362: 	if len(toolParts) > 0 {
1363: 		lines = append(lines, "**Tools:**")
1364: 		for _, tool := range toolParts {
1365: 			// Format tool details for better debugging
1366: 			toolDesc := formatToolDescription(&tool)
1367: 			lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  - %s", toolDesc))
1368: 		}
1369: 		lines = append(lines, "")
1370: 	}
1371: 
1372: 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
1373: }
1374: 
1375: // formatToolDescription formats a tool part into a human-readable string.
1376: // Shows tool name, title/description, and key parameters for debugging.
1377: func formatToolDescription(tool *MessagePart) string {
1378: 	// If no tool details available, fall back to type
1379: 	if tool.Tool == "" {
1380: 		return tool.Type
1381: 	}
1382: 
1383: 	// Start with tool name
1384: 	result := tool.Tool
1385: 
1386: 	// Add title/description if available (most useful for bash commands)
1387: 	if tool.State != nil && tool.State.Title != "" {
1388: 		result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, tool.State.Title)
1389: 	} else if tool.State != nil && len(tool.State.Input) > 0 {
1390: 		// If no title, try to extract a useful parameter
1391: 		// For common tools, show the most relevant parameter
1392: 		switch tool.Tool {
1393: 		case "read":
1394: 			if filePath, ok := tool.State.Input["filePath"].(string); ok {
1395: 				// Show just filename, not full path, to keep it concise
1396: 				filename := filePath
1397: 				if idx := strings.LastIndex(filePath, "/"); idx >= 0 && idx < len(filePath)-1 {
1398: 					filename = filePath[idx+1:]
1399: 				}
1400: 				result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, filename)
1401: 			}
1402: 		case "edit", "write":
1403: 			if filePath, ok := tool.State.Input["filePath"].(string); ok {
1404: 				filename := filePath
1405: 				if idx := strings.LastIndex(filePath, "/"); idx >= 0 && idx < len(filePath)-1 {
1406: 					filename = filePath[idx+1:]
1407: 				}
1408: 				result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, filename)
1409: 			}
1410: 		case "bash":
1411: 			if command, ok := tool.State.Input["command"].(string); ok {
1412: 				// Truncate long commands to 60 chars
1413: 				if len(command) > 60 {
1414: 					command = command[:57] + "..."
1415: 				}
1416: 				result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, command)
1417: 			}
1418: 		case "grep", "glob":
1419: 			if pattern, ok := tool.State.Input["pattern"].(string); ok {
1420: 				result = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", result, pattern)
1421: 			}
1422: 		default:
1423: 			// For other tools, just show the tool name
1424: 		}
1425: 	}
1426: 
1427: 	return result
1428: }

10: func workspacePathFromSession(session *opencode.Session) string {
11: 	if session == nil || session.Metadata == nil {
12: 		return ""
13: 	}
14: 	if workspacePath, ok := session.Metadata["workspace_path"]; ok {
15: 		return strings.TrimSpace(workspacePath)
16: 	}
17: 	return ""
18: }
19: 
20: func workspaceNameFromSession(session *opencode.Session) string {
21: 	if session == nil {
22: 		return ""
23: 	}
24: 	if workspacePath := workspacePathFromSession(session); workspacePath != "" {
25: 		return filepath.Base(workspacePath)
26: 	}
27: 	return session.Title
28: }
29: 
30: func beadsIDFromSession(session *opencode.Session) string {
31: 	if session == nil {
32: 		return ""
33: 	}
34: 	if session.Metadata != nil {
35: 		if beadsID, ok := session.Metadata["beads_id"]; ok && beadsID != "" {
36: 			return beadsID
37: 		}
38: 	}
39: 	return extractBeadsIDFromTitle(session.Title)
40: }
41: 
42: func projectDirFromWorkspacePath(workspacePath string) string {
43: 	if workspacePath == "" {
44: 		return ""
45: 	}
46: 	cleaned := filepath.Clean(workspacePath)
47: 	marker := string(filepath.Separator) + ".orch" + string(filepath.Separator) + "workspace"
48: 	idx := strings.Index(cleaned, marker)
49: 	if idx <= 0 {
50: 		return ""
51: 	}
52: 	return cleaned[:idx]

180: // isSessionLikelyProcessing checks if a session might be processing based on its last update time.
181: // Only makes the expensive IsSessionProcessing HTTP call for recently updated sessions.
182: // For sessions not updated recently, assumes they are idle (saves ~100ms per call).
183: func isSessionLikelyProcessing(client *opencode.Client, sessionID string, lastUpdated time.Time, now time.Time) bool {
184: 	// If the session hasn't been updated in the last 5 minutes, it's definitely not processing
185: 	if now.Sub(lastUpdated) > processingCheckMaxAge {
186: 		return false
187: 	}
188: 	// For recently active sessions, make the HTTP call to check processing status
189: 	return client.IsSessionProcessing(sessionID)

192: func runStatus(serverURL string) error {
193: 	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
194: 	now := time.Now()
195: 
196: 	agents := make([]AgentInfo, 0)
197: 	seenBeadsIDs := make(map[string]bool)
198: 
199: 	// Get current project's workspace directory for workspace lookups
200: 	projectDir, _ := os.Getwd()
201: 
202: 	// === OPTIMIZED: Batch fetch all data upfront ===
203: 	// 1. Fetch sessions across all known projects (not just current project).
204: 	// OpenCode scopes sessions by x-opencode-directory header, so cross-project
205: 	// agents are invisible without querying each project directory.
206: 	sessions, err := listSessionsAcrossProjects(client, projectDir)
207: 	if err != nil {
208: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to list sessions: %w", err)
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	sessionStatusMap := make(map[string]opencode.SessionStatusInfo)
212: 	if status, err := client.GetAllSessionStatus(); err == nil {
213: 		sessionStatusMap = status
214: 	} else {
215: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to fetch session status: %v\n", err)
216: 	}
217: 
218: 	// Build a map of session ID -> session for quick lookup
219: 	sessionMap := make(map[string]*opencode.Session)
220: 	// Also build a map of beadsID -> session for matching
221: 	beadsToSession := make(map[string]*opencode.Session)
222: 	const maxIdleTime = 30 * time.Minute
223: 
224: 	for i := range sessions {
225: 		s := &sessions[i]
226: 		sessionMap[s.ID] = s
227: 
228: 		// Only consider recently active sessions for beads matching
229: 		updatedAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
230: 		if now.Sub(updatedAt) <= maxIdleTime {
231: 			beadsID := beadsIDFromSession(s)
232: 			if beadsID != "" {
233: 				beadsToSession[beadsID] = s
234: 			}
235: 		}
236: 	}
237: 
238: 	// 2. Collect beads IDs first, then batch fetch issues later
239: 	// (openIssues removed - we now use allIssues to check both open and closed status)
240: 
241: 	// 3. Collect all beads IDs we need comments for
242: 	var beadsIDsToFetch []string
243: 
244: 	// Track project directories for cross-project agents (beadsID -> projectDir)
245: 	beadsProjectDirs := make(map[string]string)
246: 
247: 	// Phase 1: Collect agents from workspace AGENT_MANIFEST.json files
248: 	// Workspace files are the source of truth for spawn-time metadata.
249: 	workspaceDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "workspace")
250: 	if entries, err := os.ReadDir(workspaceDir); err == nil {
251: 		for _, entry := range entries {
252: 			if !entry.IsDir() || entry.Name() == "archived" {
253: 				continue
254: 			}
255: 
256: 			wsPath := filepath.Join(workspaceDir, entry.Name())
257: 			manifest, err := spawn.ReadAgentManifest(wsPath)
258: 			if err != nil {
259: 				continue // Skip workspaces without manifest (pre-manifest or non-agent dirs)
260: 			}
261: 
262: 			sessionID := spawn.ReadSessionID(wsPath)
263: 
264: 			info := AgentInfo{
265: 				SessionID:  sessionID,
266: 				BeadsID:    manifest.BeadsID,
267: 				Mode:       manifest.SpawnMode,
268: 				Model:      manifest.Model,
269: 				Skill:      manifest.Skill,
270: 				ProjectDir: manifest.ProjectDir,
271: 				Project:    extractProjectFromBeadsID(manifest.BeadsID),
272: 			}
273: 
274: 			// Enrich from OpenCode session if available
275: 			if sessionID != "" {
276: 				if s, ok := sessionMap[sessionID]; ok {
277: 					createdAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Created/1000, 0)
278: 					updatedAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
279: 					info.Runtime = formatDuration(now.Sub(createdAt))
280: 					info.LastActivity = updatedAt
281: 					if info.Title == "" {
282: 						info.Title = workspaceNameFromSession(s)
283: 					}
284: 					if info.ProjectDir == "" {
285: 						if projectDir := projectDirFromWorkspacePath(workspacePathFromSession(s)); projectDir != "" {
286: 							info.ProjectDir = projectDir
287: 						} else if s.Directory != "" {
288: 							info.ProjectDir = s.Directory
289: 						}
290: 					}
291: 					if statusInfo, ok := sessionStatusMap[s.ID]; ok {
292: 						info.IsProcessing = statusInfo.IsBusy() || statusInfo.IsRetrying()
293: 					} else {
294: 						info.IsProcessing = isSessionLikelyProcessing(client, s.ID, updatedAt, now)
295: 					}
296: 				} else {
297: 					info.SessionID = "api-stalled"
298: 				}
299: 			}
300: 
301: 			if manifest.BeadsID != "" {
302: 				if !seenBeadsIDs[manifest.BeadsID] {
303: 					beadsIDsToFetch = append(beadsIDsToFetch, manifest.BeadsID)
304: 					seenBeadsIDs[manifest.BeadsID] = true
305: 				}
306: 				if manifest.ProjectDir != "" {
307: 					beadsProjectDirs[manifest.BeadsID] = manifest.ProjectDir
308: 				}
309: 			}
310: 
311: 			agents = append(agents, info)
312: 		}
313: 	}
314: 
315: 	// Phase 2: Discovery - Collect agents from tmux windows (for untracked or legacy agents)
316: 	workersSessions, _ := tmux.ListWorkersSessions()
317: 	for _, sessionName := range workersSessions {
318: 		windows, _ := tmux.ListWindows(sessionName)
319: 		for _, w := range windows {
320: 			// Skip known non-agent windows
321: 			if w.Name == "servers" || w.Name == "zsh" {
322: 				continue
323: 			}
324: 
325: 			beadsID := extractBeadsIDFromWindowName(w.Name)
326: 			if beadsID == "" {

328: 			}
329:

338: 						}
339: 						break

341: 				}
342: 				continue
343: 			}
344: 
345: 			agents = append(agents, AgentInfo{
346: 				BeadsID: beadsID,
347: 				Mode:    "claude", // Legacy/untracked tmux agents are claude mode
348: 				Skill:   extractSkillFromWindowName(w.Name),
349: 				Project: extractProjectFromBeadsID(beadsID),
350: 				Window:  w.Target,
351: 				Title:   w.Name,
352: 			})
353: 
354: 			if beadsID != "" && !seenBeadsIDs[beadsID] {
355: 				beadsIDsToFetch = append(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsID)
356: 				seenBeadsIDs[beadsID] = true
357: 			}
358: 		}
359: 	}
360: 
361: 	// Phase 3: Discovery - Collect beads IDs from active OpenCode sessions (untracked)
362: 	for _, s := range sessions {
363: 		updatedAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
364: 		if now.Sub(updatedAt) > maxIdleTime {
365: 			continue
366: 		}
367: 
368: 		beadsID := extractBeadsIDFromTitle(s.Title)
369: 		if beadsID == "" {
370: 			continue
371: 		}
372: 
373: 		// Skip if already tracked via workspace scan or tmux
374: 		if seenBeadsIDs[beadsID] {
375: 			continue
376: 		}
377: 
378: 		createdAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Created/1000, 0)
379: 		// updatedAt already declared in loop, so just use existing value
380: 		agents = append(agents, AgentInfo{
381: 			SessionID:    s.ID,
382: 			BeadsID:      beadsID,
383: 			Mode:         "opencode", // Untracked OpenCode sessions are opencode mode
384: 			Title:        s.Title,
385: 			Runtime:      formatDuration(now.Sub(createdAt)),
386: 			LastActivity: updatedAt,
387: 			Skill:        extractSkillFromTitle(s.Title),
388: 			Project:      extractProjectFromBeadsID(beadsID),
389: 			IsProcessing: isSessionLikelyProcessing(client, s.ID, updatedAt, now),
390: 		})
391: 
392: 		beadsIDsToFetch = append(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsID)
393: 		seenBeadsIDs[beadsID] = true
394: 	}
395: 
396: 	// Build beadsProjectDirs map for cross-project agents.
397: 	// Strategy 1: Use session.Directory from OpenCode sessions (if valid, not "/").
398: 	// Strategy 2: Look up workspace from current project's .orch/workspace/.
399: 	// Strategy 3: Derive project directory from beads ID prefix by checking known project locations.
400: 	// This ensures we look up beads comments from the correct project's .beads/ directory.
401: 	for beadsID, session := range beadsToSession {
402: 		if session != nil && session.Directory != "" && session.Directory != "/" && session.Directory != projectDir {
403: 			beadsProjectDirs[beadsID] = session.Directory
404: 		}
405: 	}
406: 
407: 	// For beads IDs without a valid session directory, try additional strategies
408: 	for _, beadsID := range beadsIDsToFetch {
409: 		if _, hasProjectDir := beadsProjectDirs[beadsID]; hasProjectDir {
410: 			continue // Already have project dir
411: 		}
412: 
413: 		// Strategy 2: Look up workspace from current project
414: 		workspacePath, _ := findWorkspaceByBeadsID(projectDir, beadsID)
415: 		if workspacePath != "" {
416: 			agentProjectDir := extractProjectDirFromWorkspace(workspacePath)
417: 			if agentProjectDir != "" {
418: 				beadsProjectDirs[beadsID] = agentProjectDir
419: 				continue
420: 			}
421: 		}
422: 
423: 		// Strategy 3: Derive from beads ID prefix
424: 		// Beads IDs have format: project-name-xxxx (e.g., orch-go-3anf, kb-cli-xrm)
425: 		projectName := extractProjectFromBeadsID(beadsID)
426: 		if projectName != "" && projectName != "untracked" {
427: 			if derivedDir := findProjectDirByName(projectName); derivedDir != "" {
428: 				beadsProjectDirs[beadsID] = derivedDir
429: 			}
430: 		}
431: 	}
432: 
433: 	// 4. Batch fetch all comments with project-aware lookup for cross-project agents
434: 	commentsMap := verify.GetCommentsBatchWithProjectDirs(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsProjectDirs)
435: 
436: 	// 5. Batch fetch issue details to check closed status
437: 	// This also provides task info for closed issues (not returned by ListOpenIssues)
438: 	allIssues, _ := verify.GetIssuesBatch(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsProjectDirs)
439: 
440: 	// === Now enrich and filter agents ===
441: 
442: 	for i := range agents {
443: 		agent := &agents[i]
444: 
445: 		// Get phase from pre-fetched comments
446: 		if comments, ok := commentsMap[agent.BeadsID]; ok {
447: 			phaseStatus := verify.ParsePhaseFromComments(comments)
448: 			if phaseStatus.Found {
449: 				agent.Phase = phaseStatus.Phase
450: 				agent.PhaseReportedAt = phaseStatus.PhaseReportedAt
451: 			}
452: 		}
453: 
454: 		// Get task and check closed status from pre-fetched issues
455: 		beadsOpen := false
456: 		if issue, ok := allIssues[agent.BeadsID]; ok {
457: 			agent.Task = truncate(issue.Title, 40)
458: 			beadsOpen = !strings.EqualFold(issue.Status, "closed")
459: 			agent.IsCompleted = !beadsOpen
460: 		}
461: 
462: 		// Determine phantom status (match reconcile.go logic)
463: 		// Phantom = beads issue open AND no live session AND no live tmux window
464: 		opencodeLive := agent.SessionID != "" && agent.SessionID != "tmux-stalled" && agent.SessionID != "api-stalled"
465: 		tmuxLive := agent.Window != ""
466: 		agent.IsPhantom = beadsOpen && !tmuxLive && !opencodeLive
467: 
468: 		// Determine source indicator
469: 		agent.Source = determineAgentSource(*agent, projectDir)
470: 
471: 		// If it's claude mode and we matched a session, get runtime
472: 		if agent.Mode == "claude" && agent.SessionID != "" {
473: 			if s, ok := sessionMap[agent.SessionID]; ok {
474: 				createdAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Created/1000, 0)
475: 				updatedAt := time.Unix(s.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
476: 				agent.Runtime = formatDuration(now.Sub(createdAt))
477: 				if agent.Title == "" {
478: 					agent.Title = s.Title
479: 				}
480: 				agent.IsProcessing = isSessionLikelyProcessing(client, s.ID, updatedAt, now)
481: 			}
482: 		}
483: 
484: 		// Ensure runtime has a value
485: 		if agent.Runtime == "" {
486: 			agent.Runtime = "unknown"
487: 		}
488: 	}
489: 
490: 	// Phase 3: Filter agents based on flags
491: 	// Compact mode (default): Only show running agents + recently completed (Phase: Complete)
492: 	// Full mode (--all): Show all agents including idle, phantom, completed
493: 	filteredAgents := make([]AgentInfo, 0)
494: 	for _, agentItem := range agents {
495: 		// Filter by project if specified
496: 		if statusProject != "" && agentItem.Project != statusProject {
497: 			continue
498: 		}
499: 
500: 		// In compact mode, only show:
501: 		// 1. Running (processing) agents
502: 		// 2. RECENT agents with Phase: Complete (need review)
503: 		// 3. Agents with Phase: BLOCKED or QUESTION (need attention)
504: 		if !statusAll {
505: 			isRunning := agentItem.IsProcessing
506: 
507: 			isComplete := strings.EqualFold(agentItem.Phase, "Complete")
508: 			isRecent := true
509: 			if isComplete && agentItem.PhaseReportedAt != nil {
510: 				if time.Since(*agentItem.PhaseReportedAt) > compactCompletedAgentsMaxAge {
511: 					isRecent = false
512: 				}
513: 			}
514: 
515: 			needsAttention := (isComplete && isRecent) ||
516: 				strings.EqualFold(agentItem.Phase, "BLOCKED") ||
517: 				strings.EqualFold(agentItem.Phase, "QUESTION")
518: 
519: 			if !isRunning && !needsAttention {
520: 				continue // Skip idle or stale complete agents in compact mode
521: 			}
522: 		}
523: 
524: 		// Filter completed agents (beads issue closed) unless --all is set
525: 		if agentItem.IsCompleted && !statusAll {
526: 			continue
527: 		}
528: 
529: 		// Filter phantom agents unless --all is set
530: 		if agentItem.IsPhantom && !statusAll {
531: 			continue
532: 		}
533: 
534: 		filteredAgents = append(filteredAgents, agentItem)
535: 	}
536: 
537: 	// Phase 4: Build swarm status (counts before filtering)
538: 	activeCount := 0
539: 	processingCount := 0
540: 	idleCount := 0
541: 	phantomCount := 0
542: 	completedCount := 0
543: 	for _, agent := range agents {
544: 		if agent.IsPhantom {
545: 			phantomCount++
546: 		} else if agent.IsCompleted {
547: 			// Completed agents (beads issue closed) don't count as active
548: 			completedCount++
549: 		} else {
550: 			activeCount++
551: 			if agent.IsProcessing {
552: 				processingCount++
553: 			} else {
554: 				idleCount++
555: 			}
556: 		}
557: 	}
558: 
559: 	swarm := SwarmStatus{
560: 		Active:     activeCount,
561: 		Processing: processingCount,
562: 		Idle:       idleCount,
563: 		Phantom:    phantomCount,
564: 		Queued:     0,              // TODO: implement queuing system
565: 		Completed:  completedCount, // Agents with closed beads issues
566: 	}
567: 
568: 	// Fetch account usage information
569: 	accounts := getAccountUsage()
570: 
571: 	// Fetch token usage - in compact mode, only for running agents (expensive operation)
572: 	for i := range filteredAgents {
573: 		agent := &filteredAgents[i]
574: 		// Skip if no valid session ID
575: 		if agent.SessionID == "" || agent.SessionID == "tmux-stalled" {
576: 			continue
577: 		}
578: 		// In compact mode, only fetch tokens for running agents (saves ~100ms per idle agent)
579: 		if !statusAll && !agent.IsProcessing {
580: 			continue
581: 		}
582: 		tokens, err := client.GetSessionTokens(agent.SessionID)
583: 		if err == nil && tokens != nil {
584: 			agent.Tokens = tokens
585: 
586: 			// Check for stall: agent running but no token progress for N minutes
587: 			// Only check for processing agents (skip idle/phantom/completed)
588: 			if agent.IsProcessing && !agent.IsPhantom && !agent.IsCompleted {
589: 				// Use global stall tracker to detect token-based stalls
590: 				isStalled := globalStallTracker.Update(agent.SessionID, tokens)
591: 				if isStalled {
592: 					agent.IsStalled = true
593: 				}
594: 			}
595: 		}
596: 	}
597: 
598: 	// Assess context exhaustion risk - in compact mode, only for running agents
599: 	for i := range filteredAgents {
600: 		agent := &filteredAgents[i]
601: 		// Skip phantom or completed agents
602: 		if agent.IsPhantom || agent.IsCompleted {
603: 			continue
604: 		}
605: 		// In compact mode, only assess risk for running agents
606: 		if !statusAll && !agent.IsProcessing {
607: 			continue
608: 		}
609: 		// Get total tokens for risk assessment
610: 		totalTokens := 0
611: 		if agent.Tokens != nil {
612: 			totalTokens = agent.Tokens.TotalTokens
613: 			if totalTokens == 0 {
614: 				totalTokens = agent.Tokens.InputTokens + agent.Tokens.OutputTokens
615: 			}
616: 		}
617: 		// Assess risk (uses ProjectDir for git status check)
618: 		risk := verify.AssessContextRisk(totalTokens, agent.ProjectDir, agent.IsProcessing)
619: 		if risk.IsAtRisk() {
620: 			agent.ContextRisk = &risk
621: 		}
622: 	}
623: 
624: 	// Check infrastructure health
625: 	infraHealth := checkInfrastructureHealth()
626: 
627: 	// Detect synthesis opportunities - skip in compact mode (expensive filesystem scan)
628: 	var synthesisOpps *verify.SynthesisOpportunities
629: 	if statusAll {
630: 		synthesisOpps, _ = verify.DetectSynthesisOpportunities(projectDir)
631: 	}
632: 
633: 	// Get session metrics for drift detection (surfaces to Dylan)
634: 	sessionMetrics := getSessionMetrics()
635: 
636: 	// Build output (use filtered agents for display)
637: 	output := StatusOutput{
638: 		Infrastructure:         infraHealth,
639: 		SessionMetrics:         sessionMetrics,
640: 		Swarm:                  swarm,
641: 		Accounts:               accounts,
642: 		Agents:                 filteredAgents,
643: 		SynthesisOpportunities: synthesisOpps,
644: 	}
645: 
646: 	// Output as JSON if flag is set
647: 	if statusJSON {
648: 		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(output, "", "  ")
649: 		if err != nil {
650: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal JSON: %w", err)
651: 		}
652: 		fmt.Println(string(data))
653: 		return nil
654: 	}
655: 
656: 	// Print human-readable output
657: 	printSwarmStatus(output, statusAll)
658: 	return nil
659: }

661: // extractDateFromWorkspaceName parses the date suffix from a workspace name.
662: // Workspace names follow format: prefix-description-DDmon (e.g., og-feat-add-feature-24dec)
663: // Returns zero time if no valid date found.
664: func extractDateFromWorkspaceName(name string) time.Time {
665: 	// Month abbreviations (lowercase)
666: 	months := map[string]time.Month{
667: 		"jan": time.January,
668: 		"feb": time.February,
669: 		"mar": time.March,
670: 		"apr": time.April,
671: 		"may": time.May,
672: 		"jun": time.June,
673: 		"jul": time.July,
674: 		"aug": time.August,
675: 		"sep": time.September,
676: 		"oct": time.October,
677: 		"nov": time.November,
678: 		"dec": time.December,
679: 	}
680: 
681: 	// Get the last segment after the final hyphen
682: 	parts := strings.Split(name, "-")
683: 	if len(parts) == 0 {
684: 		return time.Time{}
685: 	}
686: 	lastPart := strings.ToLower(parts[len(parts)-1])
687: 
688: 	// Pattern: 1-2 digits followed by 3-letter month abbreviation (e.g., "24dec", "5jan")
689: 	if len(lastPart) < 4 || len(lastPart) > 5 {
690: 		return time.Time{}
691: 	}
692: 
693: 	// Extract the month abbreviation (last 3 chars)
694: 	monthStr := lastPart[len(lastPart)-3:]
695: 	month, ok := months[monthStr]
696: 	if !ok {
697: 		return time.Time{}
698: 	}
699: 
700: 	// Extract the day (remaining digits)
701: 	dayStr := lastPart[:len(lastPart)-3]
702: 	day, err := strconv.Atoi(dayStr)
703: 	if err != nil || day < 1 || day > 31 {
704: 		return time.Time{}
705: 	}
706: 
707: 	// Use current year, adjusting for year boundary
708: 	// (if the date is in the future within this calendar, it's probably from last year)
709: 	now := time.Now()
710: 	year := now.Year()
711: 	parsedDate := time.Date(year, month, day, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.Local)
712: 
713: 	// If the parsed date is more than a week in the future, assume it's from last year
714: 	if parsedDate.After(now.AddDate(0, 0, 7)) {
715: 		parsedDate = time.Date(year-1, month, day, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.Local)
716: 	}
717: 
718: 	return parsedDate
719: }
720: 
721: // getPhaseAndTask retrieves the current phase and task description from beads.
722: func getPhaseAndTask(beadsID string) (phase, task string) {
723: 	// Get issue for task description
724: 	issue, err := verify.GetIssue(beadsID)
725: 	if err == nil {
726: 		task = truncate(issue.Title, 40)
727: 	}
728: 
729: 	// Get phase from comments
730: 	status, err := verify.GetPhaseStatus(beadsID)
731: 	if err == nil && status.Found {
732: 		phase = status.Phase
733: 	}
734: 
735: 	return phase, task
736: }

738: // getAccountUsage fetches usage info for all configured accounts.
739: func getAccountUsage() []AccountUsage {
740: 	var accounts []AccountUsage
741: 
742: 	// Get current account usage
743: 	currentUsage := usage.FetchUsage()
744: 	if currentUsage.Error == "" && currentUsage.SevenDay != nil {
745: 		current := AccountUsage{
746: 			Name:        "current",
747: 			Email:       currentUsage.Email,
748: 			UsedPercent: currentUsage.SevenDay.Utilization,
749: 			IsActive:    true,
750: 		}
751: 		if currentUsage.SevenDay.ResetsAt != nil {
752: 			current.ResetTime = currentUsage.SevenDay.TimeUntilReset()
753: 		}
754: 		accounts = append(accounts, current)
755: 	}
756: 
757: 	// Try to get saved accounts info (without switching)
758: 	cfg, err := account.LoadConfig()
759: 	if err == nil {
760: 		for name, acc := range cfg.Accounts {
761: 			if acc.Source == "saved" {
762: 				// Check if this is the current account (by email match)
763: 				isCurrentAccount := false
764: 				for i := range accounts {
765: 					if accounts[i].Email == acc.Email {
766: 						accounts[i].Name = name // Update name to the saved account name
767: 						isCurrentAccount = true
768: 						break
769: 					}
770: 				}
771: 				if !isCurrentAccount {
772: 					// Add as a saved account (no live usage data without switching)
773: 					accounts = append(accounts, AccountUsage{
774: 						Name:     name,
775: 						Email:    acc.Email,
776: 						IsActive: false,
777: 					})
778: 				}
779: 			}
780: 		}
781: 	}
782: 
783: 	return accounts
784: }
785: 
786: // getSessionMetrics computes drift detection metrics for the current orchestrator session.
787: // Returns nil if no session is active (which is itself a signal for Dylan).
788: func getSessionMetrics() *SessionMetrics {
789: 	store, err := session.New("")
790: 	if err != nil {
791: 		return nil // Can't access session state
792: 	}
793: 
794: 	if !store.IsActive() {
795: 		// No active session - return minimal metrics to surface this
796: 		return &SessionMetrics{
797: 			HasActiveSession: false,
798: 			TimeInSession:    "-",
799: 		}
800: 	}
801: 
802: 	sess := store.Get()
803: 	if sess == nil {
804: 		return nil
805: 	}
806: 
807: 	now := time.Now()
808: 	lastActivity := sess.StartedAt
809: 	if len(sess.Spawns) > 0 {
810: 		lastActivity = sess.Spawns[len(sess.Spawns)-1].SpawnedAt
811: 	}
812: 	if now.Sub(lastActivity) > sessionMetricsStaleAfter {
813: 		return &SessionMetrics{
814: 			HasActiveSession: false,
815: 			TimeInSession:    "-",
816: 		}
817: 	}
818: 	metrics := &SessionMetrics{
819: 		HasActiveSession: true,
820: 		Goal:             sess.Goal,
821: 		SpawnCount:       len(sess.Spawns),
822: 		TimeInSession:    formatDuration(now.Sub(sess.StartedAt)),
823: 	}
824: 
825: 	// Calculate time since last spawn
826: 	if len(sess.Spawns) > 0 {
827: 		lastSpawn := sess.Spawns[len(sess.Spawns)-1]
828: 		metrics.TimeSinceLastSpawn = formatDuration(now.Sub(lastSpawn.SpawnedAt))
829: 	}
830: 
831: 	// NOTE: FileReadsSinceLastSpawn is not yet implemented.
832: 	// Would require tracking orchestrator tool usage via OpenCode events or plugins.
833: 	// For now, this field remains 0 (omitted in JSON).
834: 
835: 	return metrics
836: }

838: // Terminal width thresholds for adaptive output
839: const (
840: 	termWidthWide   = 120 // Full table with all columns
841: 	termWidthNarrow = 100 // Drop TASK column, abbreviate SKILL
842: 	termWidthMin    = 80  // Minimum supported width (vertical card format)
843: )
844: 
845: // getTerminalWidth returns the current terminal width, or a default if detection fails.
846: // Returns the width and whether we're outputting to a real terminal.
847: func getTerminalWidth() (int, bool) {
848: 	width, _, err := term.GetSize(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
849: 	if err != nil {
850: 		// Not a terminal (piped output) - use wide format
851: 		return termWidthWide + 1, false
852: 	}
853: 	return width, true
854: }
855: 
856: // printSwarmStatus prints the swarm status in human-readable format.
857: // Adapts output format based on terminal width.
858: func printSwarmStatus(output StatusOutput, showAll bool) {
859: 	width, _ := getTerminalWidth()
860: 	printSwarmStatusWithWidth(output, showAll, width)
861: }
862: 
863: // printSwarmStatusWithWidth prints swarm status with explicit width (for testing).
864: func printSwarmStatusWithWidth(output StatusOutput, showAll bool, termWidth int) {
865: 	// Check for dev mode and warn
866: 	if devModeInfo, err := readDevModeFile(".dev-mode"); err == nil {
867: 		duration := time.Since(devModeInfo.Started).Round(time.Minute)
868: 		fmt.Printf("WARNING: DEV MODE ACTIVE (%s): %s\n", duration, devModeInfo.Reason)
869: 		fmt.Println("   Infrastructure is unprotected. Run 'orch mode ops' when done.")
870: 		fmt.Println()
871: 	}
872: 
873: 	// Print infrastructure health section first
874: 	printInfrastructureHealth(output.Infrastructure)
875: 
876: 	// Print session metrics for drift detection (surfaces to Dylan)
877: 	// Position: After infrastructure, before swarm - visible at top
878: 	printSessionMetrics(output.SessionMetrics)
879: 
880: 	// Print swarm summary header with processing breakdown
881: 	fmt.Printf("SWARM STATUS: Active: %d", output.Swarm.Active)
882: 	if output.Swarm.Active > 0 {
883: 		fmt.Printf(" (running: %d, idle: %d)", output.Swarm.Processing, output.Swarm.Idle)
884: 	}
885: 	if output.Swarm.Completed > 0 {
886: 		fmt.Printf(", Completed: %d", output.Swarm.Completed)
887: 		if !showAll {
888: 			fmt.Printf(" (use --all to show)")
889: 		}
890: 	}
891: 	if output.Swarm.Phantom > 0 {
892: 		fmt.Printf(", Phantom: %d", output.Swarm.Phantom)
893: 		if !showAll {
894: 			fmt.Printf(" (use --all to show)")
895: 		}
896: 	}
897: 	fmt.Println()
898: 	// In compact mode, add hint about hidden idle agents
899: 	if !showAll && output.Swarm.Idle > 0 && output.Swarm.Idle > len(output.Agents) {
900: 		hiddenIdle := output.Swarm.Idle - countIdleInList(output.Agents)
901: 		if hiddenIdle > 0 {
902: 			fmt.Printf("  (compact mode: %d idle agents hidden, use --all for full list)\n", hiddenIdle)
903: 		}
904: 	}
905: 	fmt.Println()
906: 
907: 	// Print account usage
908: 	if len(output.Accounts) > 0 {
909: 		fmt.Println("ACCOUNTS")
910: 		for _, acc := range output.Accounts {
911: 			activeMarker := ""
912: 			if acc.IsActive {
913: 				activeMarker = " *"
914: 			}
915: 			usageStr := "N/A"
916: 			if acc.UsedPercent > 0 || acc.IsActive {
917: 				usageStr = fmt.Sprintf("%.0f%% used", acc.UsedPercent)
918: 				if acc.ResetTime != "" {
919: 					usageStr += fmt.Sprintf(" (resets in %s)", acc.ResetTime)
920: 				}
921: 			}
922: 			name := acc.Name
923: 			if acc.Email != "" && acc.Name == "current" {
924: 				name = acc.Email
925: 			}
926: 			fmt.Printf("  %-20s %s%s\n", name+":", usageStr, activeMarker)
927: 		}
928: 		fmt.Println()
929: 	}
930: 
931: 	// Print agents in format appropriate for terminal width
932: 	if len(output.Agents) > 0 {
933: 		fmt.Println("AGENTS")
934: 		if termWidth < termWidthMin {
935: 			printAgentsCardFormat(output.Agents)
936: 		} else if termWidth < termWidthNarrow {
937: 			printAgentsNarrowFormat(output.Agents)
938: 		} else {
939: 			printAgentsWideFormat(output.Agents)
940: 		}
941: 	} else {
942: 		fmt.Println("No active agents")
943: 	}
944: 
945: 	// Print synthesis opportunities (if any)
946: 	if output.SynthesisOpportunities != nil && output.SynthesisOpportunities.HasOpportunities() {

1134: // countIdleInList counts the number of idle agents in a list.
1135: // Used to calculate how many idle agents are hidden in compact mode.
1136: func countIdleInList(agents []AgentInfo) int {
1137: 	count := 0
1138: 	for _, agent := range agents {
1139: 		if !agent.IsProcessing && !agent.IsPhantom && !agent.IsCompleted {
1140: 			count++
1141: 		}
1142: 	}
1143: 	return count

1146: // getAgentStatus returns a status string based on agent state.
1147: func getAgentStatus(agent AgentInfo) string {
1148: 	if agent.IsCompleted {
1149: 		return "completed"
1150: 	}
1151: 	if agent.IsPhantom {
1152: 		return "phantom"
1153: 	}
1154: 	if agent.IsStalled {
1155: 		return "⚠️ STALLED"
1156: 	}
1157: 	if agent.IsProcessing {
1158: 		return "running"
1159: 	}
1160: 	return "idle"
1161: }

1163: // determineAgentSource returns the primary source indicator for an agent.
1164: // Priority: T (tmux) > O (OpenCode) > B (beads phantom) > W (workspace).
1165: // Returns: T=tmux, O=opencode, B=beads phantom, W=workspace, or empty string if unknown.
1166: func determineAgentSource(agent AgentInfo, projectDir string) string {
1167: 	// Tmux has highest priority (visible TUI)
1168: 	if agent.Window != "" {
1169: 		return "T"
1170: 	}
1171: 
1172: 	// OpenCode session (headless or API mode)
1173: 	if agent.SessionID != "" && agent.SessionID != "tmux-stalled" && agent.SessionID != "api-stalled" {
1174: 		return "O"
1175: 	}
1176: 
1177: 	// Beads phantom (issue exists but no active runtime)
1178: 	if agent.BeadsID != "" && agent.IsPhantom {
1179: 		return "B"
1180: 	}
1181: 
1182: 	// Workspace (has workspace directory)
1183: 	if agent.BeadsID != "" && projectDir != "" {
1184: 		workspacePath, _ := findWorkspaceByBeadsID(projectDir, agent.BeadsID)
1185: 		if workspacePath != "" {
1186: 			return "W"
1187: 		}
1188: 	}
1189: 
1190: 	return ""
1191: }

1202: // printSessionMetrics prints the session metrics section for drift detection.
1203: // Purpose: Surface orchestrator behavior to Dylan so he can detect drift
1204: // (e.g., "2 hours, no spawns" → orchestrator doing task work instead of delegating).
1205: func printSessionMetrics(metrics *SessionMetrics) {
1206: 	if metrics == nil {
1207: 		return
1208: 	}
1209: 
1210: 	fmt.Println("SESSION METRICS")
1211: 
1212: 	if !metrics.HasActiveSession {
1213: 		fmt.Println("  No active session (run 'orch session start \"goal\"')")
1214: 		fmt.Println()
1215: 		return
1216: 	}
1217: 
1218: 	// Print goal first (context for the numbers)
1219: 	if metrics.Goal != "" {
1220: 		fmt.Printf("  Goal: %s\n", truncate(metrics.Goal, 50))
1221: 	}
1222: 
1223: 	// Core metrics for drift detection
1224: 	fmt.Printf("  Time in session: %s\n", metrics.TimeInSession)
1225: 
1226: 	if metrics.SpawnCount > 0 {
1227: 		fmt.Printf("  Last spawn: %s ago\n", metrics.TimeSinceLastSpawn)
1228: 	} else {
1229: 		fmt.Printf("  Last spawn: no spawns yet\n")
1230: 	}
1231: 
1232: 	fmt.Printf("  Spawns: %d\n", metrics.SpawnCount)
1233: 
1234: 	// File reads metric (placeholder for future implementation)
1235: 	// NOTE: Not surfacing FileReadsSinceLastSpawn until tracking is implemented
1236: 
1237: 	fmt.Println()
1238: }

1291: // formatTokenCount formats a token count with K/M suffixes for readability.
1292: func formatTokenCount(count int) string {
1293: 	if count < 1000 {
1294: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%d", count)
1295: 	}
1296: 	if count < 1000000 {
1297: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%.1fK", float64(count)/1000)
1298: 	}
1299: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%.1fM", float64(count)/1000000)
1300: }

1302: // formatTokenStats returns a formatted string of token usage.
1303: func formatTokenStats(tokens *opencode.TokenStats) string {
1304: 	if tokens == nil {
1305: 		return "-"
1306: 	}
1307: 	// Format: "in:X out:Y (cache:Z)"
1308: 	result := fmt.Sprintf("in:%s out:%s", formatTokenCount(tokens.InputTokens), formatTokenCount(tokens.OutputTokens))
1309: 	if tokens.CacheReadTokens > 0 {
1310: 		result += fmt.Sprintf(" (cache:%s)", formatTokenCount(tokens.CacheReadTokens))
1311: 	}
1312: 	return result
1313: }
1314: 
1315: // formatTokenStatsCompact returns a compact formatted string of token usage for table display.
1316: // Shows total tokens with input/output breakdown: "12.5K (8K/4K)"
1317: func formatTokenStatsCompact(tokens *opencode.TokenStats) string {
1318: 	if tokens == nil {
1319: 		return "-"
1320: 	}
1321: 	total := tokens.TotalTokens
1322: 	if total == 0 {
1323: 		total = tokens.InputTokens + tokens.OutputTokens
1324: 	}
1325: 	if total == 0 {
1326: 		return "-"
1327: 	}
1328: 	// Format: "total (in/out)" for quick scanning
1329: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s/%s)",
1330: 		formatTokenCount(total),
1331: 		formatTokenCount(tokens.InputTokens),
1332: 		formatTokenCount(tokens.OutputTokens))
1333: }

1335: // getBeadsIssuePrefix reads the issue_prefix for a project using bd CLI.
1336: // Returns empty string if the command fails or project doesn't have beads.
1337: func getBeadsIssuePrefix(projectPath string) string {
1338: 	cmd := exec.Command("bd", "config", "get", "issue_prefix")
1339: 	cmd.Dir = projectPath
1340: 	output, err := cmd.Output()
1341: 	if err != nil {
1342: 		return ""
1343: 	}
1344: 
1345: 	// Output is just the value (e.g., "pw\n")
1346: 	return strings.TrimSpace(string(output))
1347: }

1349: // getKBProjectsWithNames fetches registered projects from kb with name and path.
1350: // Returns empty slice if kb is unavailable or fails (graceful degradation).
1351: func getKBProjectsWithNames() []kbProject {
1352: 	cmd := exec.Command("kb", "projects", "list", "--json")
1353: 	output, err := cmd.Output()
1354: 	if err != nil {
1355: 		return []kbProject{}
1356: 	}
1357: 
1358: 	var projects []kbProject
1359: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &projects); err != nil {
1360: 		return []kbProject{}
1361: 	}
1362: 
1363: 	return projects
1364: }

1366: // findProjectByBeadsPrefix searches for a project with the given beads issue prefix.
1367: // First checks kb's project registry, then falls back to standard locations.
1368: // Returns the project directory path, or empty string if not found.
1369: func findProjectByBeadsPrefix(prefix string) string {
1370: 	// Try kb's project registry first
1371: 	for _, project := range getKBProjectsWithNames() {
1372: 		if projectPrefix := getBeadsIssuePrefix(project.Path); projectPrefix == prefix {
1373: 			return project.Path
1374: 		}
1375: 	}
1376: 
1377: 	// Fall back to checking standard locations
1378: 	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
1379: 	if err != nil {
1380: 		return ""
1381: 	}
1382: 
1383: 	candidatePaths := []string{
1384: 		filepath.Join(homeDir, "Documents", "personal", prefix),
1385: 		filepath.Join(homeDir, prefix),
1386: 		filepath.Join(homeDir, "projects", prefix),
1387: 		filepath.Join(homeDir, "src", prefix),
1388: 	}
1389: 
1390: 	for _, path := range candidatePaths {
1391: 		if projectPrefix := getBeadsIssuePrefix(path); projectPrefix == prefix {
1392: 			return path
1393: 		}
1394: 	}
1395: 
1396: 	return ""
1397: }
1398: 
1399: // findProjectDirByName looks up a project directory by its name or beads prefix.
1400: // First checks kb's project registry, then searches common project locations.
1401: // Verifies the project has a .beads/ directory.
1402: // Returns empty string if not found.
1403: func findProjectDirByName(projectName string) string {
1404: 	// Try kb's project registry first (handles non-standard locations)
1405: 	for _, project := range getKBProjectsWithNames() {
1406: 		if project.Name == projectName {
1407: 			// Verify it has a .beads directory
1408: 			beadsPath := filepath.Join(project.Path, ".beads")
1409: 			if info, err := os.Stat(beadsPath); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
1410: 				return project.Path
1411: 			}
1412: 		}
1413: 	}
1414: 
1415: 	// If projectName looks like a beads prefix (short, no hyphens except separators),
1416: 	// try finding by prefix instead
1417: 	if len(projectName) <= 10 && !strings.Contains(projectName, "/") {
1418: 		if path := findProjectByBeadsPrefix(projectName); path != "" {
1419: 			return path
1420: 		}
1421: 	}
1422: 
1423: 	// Fall back to checking standard locations
1424: 	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
1425: 	if err != nil {
1426: 		return ""
1427: 	}
1428: 
1429: 	// Common project locations in order of priority
1430: 	candidatePaths := []string{
1431: 		filepath.Join(homeDir, "Documents", "personal", projectName),
1432: 		filepath.Join(homeDir, projectName),
1433: 		filepath.Join(homeDir, "projects", projectName),
1434: 		filepath.Join(homeDir, "src", projectName),
1435: 	}
1436: 
1437: 	for _, path := range candidatePaths {
1438: 		// Check if directory exists and has .beads/ (confirms it's a beads-tracked project)
1439: 		beadsPath := filepath.Join(path, ".beads")
1440: 		if info, err := os.Stat(beadsPath); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
1441: 			return path
1442: 		}
1443: 	}
1444: 
1445: 	return ""
1446: }

1448: // checkInfrastructureHealth checks the health of infrastructure services.
1449: // Performs TCP connect tests for dashboard (port 3348) and OpenCode (port 4096),
1450: // and reads daemon status from ~/.orch/daemon-status.json.
1451: func checkInfrastructureHealth() *InfrastructureHealth {
1452: 	health := &InfrastructureHealth{
1453: 		AllHealthy: true,
1454: 		Services:   make([]InfraServiceStatus, 0, 2),
1455: 	}
1456: 
1457: 	// Check Dashboard server (orch serve) on port 3348
1458: 	dashboardStatus := checkTCPPort("Dashboard", DefaultServePort)
1459: 	health.Services = append(health.Services, dashboardStatus)
1460: 	if !dashboardStatus.Running {
1461: 		health.AllHealthy = false
1462: 	}
1463: 
1464: 	// Check OpenCode server on port 4096
1465: 	opencodeStatus := checkTCPPort("OpenCode", 4096)
1466: 	health.Services = append(health.Services, opencodeStatus)
1467: 	if !opencodeStatus.Running {
1468: 		health.AllHealthy = false
1469: 	}
1470: 
1471: 	// Check daemon status from file
1472: 	daemonStatus := readDaemonStatus()
1473: 	health.Daemon = daemonStatus
1474: 	if daemonStatus == nil || daemonStatus.Status != "running" {
1475: 		health.AllHealthy = false
1476: 	}
1477: 
1478: 	return health
1479: }
1480: 
1481: // checkTCPPort performs a TCP connect test to verify a service is listening.
1482: func checkTCPPort(name string, port int) InfraServiceStatus {
1483: 	status := InfraServiceStatus{
1484: 		Name: name,
1485: 		Port: port,
1486: 	}
1487: 
1488: 	addr := fmt.Sprintf("localhost:%d", port)
1489: 	conn, err := tcpDialTimeout(addr, 1*time.Second)
1490: 	if err != nil {
1491: 		status.Running = false
1492: 		status.Details = "not responding"
1493: 		return status
1494: 	}
1495: 	conn.Close()
1496: 
1497: 	status.Running = true
1498: 	status.Details = "listening"
1499: 	return status
1500: }
1501: 
1502: // tcpDialTimeout dials a TCP address with a timeout.
1503: // This is a wrapper to allow for testing.

522: 		}
523: 
524: 		// Merge beadsToProjectDir map
525: 		for beadsID, projDir := range result.cache.beadsToProjectDir {
526: 			if _, exists := merged.beadsToProjectDir[beadsID]; !exists {
527: 				merged.beadsToProjectDir[beadsID] = projDir
528: 			}
529: 		}
530: 
531: 		// Merge workspaceEntryToPath map (for multi-project workspace path resolution)
532: 		for entryName, wsPath := range result.cache.workspaceEntryToPath {
533: 			if _, exists := merged.workspaceEntryToPath[entryName]; !exists {
534: 				merged.workspaceEntryToPath[entryName] = wsPath
535: 			}
536: 		}
537: 
538: 		// Merge workspace entries (for completed workspace scanning)
539: 		merged.workspaceEntries = append(merged.workspaceEntries, result.cache.workspaceEntries...)
540: 
541: 		// Keep track of workspace dir for backward compatibility
542: 		// (use first non-empty workspace dir)
543: 		if merged.workspaceDir == "" && result.cache.workspaceDir != "" {
544: 			merged.workspaceDir = result.cache.workspaceDir
545: 		}
546: 	}
547: 
548: 	return merged
549: }
550: 
551: // buildWorkspaceCache scans the workspace directory once and builds lookup maps.
552: // This replaces multiple calls to findWorkspaceByBeadsID which each scanned all 400+ directories.
553: func buildWorkspaceCache(projectDir string) *workspaceCache {
554: 	cache := &workspaceCache{
555: 		beadsToWorkspace:     make(map[string]string),
556: 		beadsToProjectDir:    make(map[string]string),
557: 		workspaceDir:         filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "workspace"),
558: 		workspaceEntryToPath: make(map[string]string),
559: 	}
560: 
561: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(cache.workspaceDir)
562: 	if err != nil {
563: 		return cache // Empty cache if directory doesn't exist
564: 	}
565: 	cache.workspaceEntries = entries
566: 
567: 	// Single scan: extract beads ID and project dir from each workspace
568: 	for _, entry := range entries {
569: 		if !entry.IsDir() {
570: 			continue
571: 		}
572: 
573: 		dirName := entry.Name()
574: 		dirPath := filepath.Join(cache.workspaceDir, dirName)
575: 		spawnContextPath := filepath.Join(dirPath, "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md")
576: 
577: 		// Store entry name to absolute path mapping for multi-project support
578: 		cache.workspaceEntryToPath[dirName] = dirPath
579: 
580: 		// Read SPAWN_CONTEXT.md once to extract both beads ID and PROJECT_DIR
581: 		content, err := os.ReadFile(spawnContextPath)
582: 		if err != nil {
583: 			continue // Skip workspaces without SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
584: 		}
585: 		contentStr := string(content)
586: 
587: 		var beadsID, agentProjectDir string
588: 
589: 		// Parse once, extracting both pieces of info
590: 		for _, line := range strings.Split(contentStr, "\n") {
591: 			lineTrimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
592: 
593: 			// Extract beads ID from "spawned from beads issue: **xxx**" or "bd comment xxx"
594: 			if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(line), "spawned from beads issue:") {
595: 				// Pattern: "spawned from beads issue: **orch-go-xxxx**"
596: 				// Extract the beads ID between ** markers or after the colon
597: 				if idx := strings.Index(line, "**"); idx != -1 {
598: 					rest := line[idx+2:]
599: 					if endIdx := strings.Index(rest, "**"); endIdx != -1 {
600: 						beadsID = rest[:endIdx]
601: 					}
602: 				}
603: 			} else if strings.HasPrefix(lineTrimmed, "bd comment ") {
604: 				// Pattern: "bd comment orch-go-xxxx ..."
605: 				parts := strings.Fields(lineTrimmed)
606: 				if len(parts) >= 3 {
607: 					beadsID = parts[2]
608: 				}
609: 			}
610: 
611: 			// Extract PROJECT_DIR
612: 			if strings.HasPrefix(lineTrimmed, "PROJECT_DIR:") {
613: 				agentProjectDir = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(lineTrimmed, "PROJECT_DIR:"))
614: 			}
615: 		}
616: 
617: 		// Store in cache if beads ID found
618: 		if beadsID != "" {
619: 			cache.beadsToWorkspace[beadsID] = dirPath
620: 			if agentProjectDir != "" {
621: 				cache.beadsToProjectDir[beadsID] = agentProjectDir
622: 			}
623: 		}
624: 	}
625: 
626: 	return cache
627: }
628: 
629: // lookupWorkspace returns the workspace path for a beads ID (O(1) lookup).
630: func (c *workspaceCache) lookupWorkspace(beadsID string) string {
631: 	return c.beadsToWorkspace[beadsID]
632: }
633: 
634: // lookupProjectDir returns the PROJECT_DIR for a beads ID (O(1) lookup).
635: func (c *workspaceCache) lookupProjectDir(beadsID string) string {
636: 	return c.beadsToProjectDir[beadsID]
637: }
638: 
639: // lookupWorkspacePathByEntry returns the absolute workspace path for a directory entry name.
640: // This is used in multi-project scenarios where workspace entries come from different projects.
641: func (c *workspaceCache) lookupWorkspacePathByEntry(entryName string) string {
642: 	if path, ok := c.workspaceEntryToPath[entryName]; ok {
643: 		return path
644: 	}
645: 	// Fallback to single-project path construction
646: 	if c.workspaceDir != "" {
647: 		return filepath.Join(c.workspaceDir, entryName)
648: 	}
649: 	return ""
650: }

(End of file - total 650 lines)
</content>

16: // SessionIDFilename is the name of the file storing the session ID in the workspace.
17: const SessionIDFilename = ".session_id"
18: 
19: // WriteSessionID writes the OpenCode session ID to the workspace directory.
20: // Uses atomic write (temp file + rename) to prevent partial reads.
21: // The workspace directory must already exist.
22: func WriteSessionID(workspacePath, sessionID string) error {
23: 	if sessionID == "" {
24: 		return nil // Nothing to write
25: 	}
26: 
27: 	sessionFile := filepath.Join(workspacePath, SessionIDFilename)
28: 	tmpFile := sessionFile + ".tmp"
29: 
30: 	// Write to temp file first
31: 	if err := os.WriteFile(tmpFile, []byte(sessionID+"\n"), 0644); err != nil {
32: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write session ID temp file: %w", err)
33: 	}
34: 
35: 	// Atomic rename
36: 	if err := os.Rename(tmpFile, sessionFile); err != nil {
37: 		os.Remove(tmpFile) // Clean up temp file on error
38: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to rename session ID file: %w", err)
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	return nil
42: }
43: 
44: // ReadSessionID reads the OpenCode session ID from the workspace directory.
45: // Returns empty string if the file doesn't exist or is empty.
46: func ReadSessionID(workspacePath string) string {
47: 	sessionFile := filepath.Join(workspacePath, SessionIDFilename)
48: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(sessionFile)
49: 	if err != nil {
50: 		return ""
51: 	}
52: 	return strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
53: }
54: 
55: // SessionIDPath returns the path to the session ID file for a workspace.
56: func SessionIDPath(workspacePath string) string {

58: }

60: // TierFilename is the name of the file storing the spawn tier in the workspace.
61: const TierFilename = ".tier"
62: 
63: // WriteTier writes the spawn tier to the workspace directory.
64: // Uses atomic write (temp file + rename) to prevent partial reads.
65: // The workspace directory must already exist.
66: func WriteTier(workspacePath, tier string) error {
67: 	if tier == "" {
68: 		return nil // Nothing to write
69: 	}
70: 
71: 	tierFile := filepath.Join(workspacePath, TierFilename)
72: 	tmpFile := tierFile + ".tmp"
73: 
74: 	// Write to temp file first
75: 	if err := os.WriteFile(tmpFile, []byte(tier+"\n"), 0644); err != nil {
76: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write tier temp file: %w", err)
77: 	}
78: 
79: 	// Atomic rename
80: 	if err := os.Rename(tmpFile, tierFile); err != nil {
81: 		os.Remove(tmpFile) // Clean up temp file on error
82: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to rename tier file: %w", err)
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	return nil
86: }
87: 
88: // ReadTier reads the spawn tier from the workspace directory.
89: // Returns empty string if the file doesn't exist or is empty.
90: // Returns TierFull as the default if the file is missing (conservative default).
91: func ReadTier(workspacePath string) string {
92: 	tierFile := filepath.Join(workspacePath, TierFilename)
93: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(tierFile)
94: 	if err != nil {
95: 		// Conservative default: return TierFull for old workspaces without tier file
96: 		return TierFull
97: 	}
98: 	tier := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
99: 	if tier == "" {

104: 
105: // TierPath returns the path to the tier file for a workspace.

108: }
109: 
110: // SpawnTimeFilename is the name of the file storing the spawn timestamp in the workspace.
111: const SpawnTimeFilename = ".spawn_time"
112: 
113: // WriteSpawnTime writes the spawn timestamp to the workspace directory.
114: // The timestamp is stored as Unix epoch nanoseconds for precision.
115: // Uses atomic write (temp file + rename) to prevent partial reads.
116: // The workspace directory must already exist.
117: func WriteSpawnTime(workspacePath string, t time.Time) error {
118: 	spawnTimeFile := filepath.Join(workspacePath, SpawnTimeFilename)
119: 	tmpFile := spawnTimeFile + ".tmp"
120: 
121: 	// Store as Unix nanoseconds for precision
122: 	content := strconv.FormatInt(t.UnixNano(), 10) + "\n"
123: 
124: 	// Write to temp file first
125: 	if err := os.WriteFile(tmpFile, []byte(content), 0644); err != nil {
126: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write spawn time temp file: %w", err)
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	// Atomic rename
130: 	if err := os.Rename(tmpFile, spawnTimeFile); err != nil {
131: 		os.Remove(tmpFile) // Clean up temp file on error
132: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to rename spawn time file: %w", err)
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	return nil
136: }
137: 
138: // ReadSpawnTime reads the spawn timestamp from the workspace directory.
139: // Returns zero time if the file doesn't exist or is invalid.
140: func ReadSpawnTime(workspacePath string) time.Time {
141: 	spawnTimeFile := filepath.Join(workspacePath, SpawnTimeFilename)
142: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(spawnTimeFile)
143: 	if err != nil {
144: 		return time.Time{} // Return zero time if file doesn't exist
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	nanos, err := strconv.ParseInt(strings.TrimSpace(string(data)), 10, 64)
148: 	if err != nil {
149: 		return time.Time{} // Return zero time if parse fails
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	return time.Unix(0, nanos)
153: }
154: 
155: // SpawnTimePath returns the path to the spawn time file for a workspace.
156: func SpawnTimePath(workspacePath string) string {
157: 	return filepath.Join(workspacePath, SpawnTimeFilename)
158: }
159: 
160: // AgentManifestFilename is the name of the file storing the agent manifest in the workspace.
161: const AgentManifestFilename = "AGENT_MANIFEST.json"
162: 
163: // AgentManifest contains canonical agent identity and spawn-time metadata.
164: // This provides a single source of truth for "what did this agent do" to enable
165: // reliable git-based scoping for verification gates.
166: //
167: // See .kb/investigations/2026-01-17-inv-design-synthesize-26-completion-investigations.md
168: // for the architectural rationale.
169: type AgentManifest struct {
170: 	// WorkspaceName is the canonical agent identifier (e.g., "og-feat-add-manifest-17jan-abc1")
171: 	WorkspaceName string `json:"workspace_name"`
172: 
173: 	// Skill is the skill used to spawn this agent (e.g., "feature-impl", "investigation")
174: 	Skill string `json:"skill"`
175: 
176: 	// BeadsID is the beads issue ID for tracking (empty for --no-track spawns)
177: 	BeadsID string `json:"beads_id,omitempty"`
178: 
179: 	// ProjectDir is the absolute path to the project directory
180: 	ProjectDir string `json:"project_dir"`
181: 
182: 	// GitBaseline is the git commit SHA at spawn time
183: 	// Used for git-based change detection: git diff <baseline>..HEAD
184: 	// Empty if not in a git repository or git command fails
185: 	GitBaseline string `json:"git_baseline,omitempty"`
186: 
187: 	// SpawnTime is the ISO 8601 timestamp when the agent was spawned
188: 	SpawnTime string `json:"spawn_time"`
189: 
190: 	// Tier is the spawn tier: "light" or "full"
191: 	Tier string `json:"tier"`
192: 
193: 	// SpawnMode is the spawn backend: "opencode" or "claude"
194: 	SpawnMode string `json:"spawn_mode,omitempty"`
195: 
196: 	// Model is the model spec used for this agent (e.g., "gemini-3-flash-preview", "claude-opus-4-5-20251101")
197: 	Model string `json:"model,omitempty"`
198: 
199: 	// SessionID is the OpenCode session ID (empty for claude backend which has no OpenCode session)
200: 	SessionID string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
201: }
202: 
203: // WriteAgentManifest writes the agent manifest JSON to the workspace directory.
204: // The manifest provides a canonical source of agent identity and spawn-time context.
205: // Uses atomic write (temp file + rename) to prevent partial reads.
206: // The workspace directory must already exist.
207: func WriteAgentManifest(workspacePath string, manifest AgentManifest) error {
208: 	manifestFile := filepath.Join(workspacePath, AgentManifestFilename)
209: 	tmpFile := manifestFile + ".tmp"
210: 
211: 	// Marshal to JSON with indentation for human readability
212: 	data, err := json.MarshalIndent(manifest, "", "  ")
213: 	if err != nil {
214: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal manifest: %w", err)
215: 	}
216: 
217: 	// Add trailing newline for POSIX compliance
218: 	data = append(data, '\n')
219: 
220: 	// Write to temp file first
221: 	if err := os.WriteFile(tmpFile, data, 0644); err != nil {
222: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write manifest temp file: %w", err)
223: 	}
224: 
225: 	// Atomic rename
226: 	if err := os.Rename(tmpFile, manifestFile); err != nil {
227: 		os.Remove(tmpFile) // Clean up temp file on error
228: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to rename manifest file: %w", err)
229: 	}
230: 
231: 	return nil
232: }
233: 
234: // ReadAgentManifest reads the agent manifest from the workspace directory.
235: // Returns an error if the file doesn't exist or is malformed.
236: func ReadAgentManifest(workspacePath string) (*AgentManifest, error) {
237: 	manifestFile := filepath.Join(workspacePath, AgentManifestFilename)
238: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(manifestFile)
239: 	if err != nil {
240: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read manifest: %w", err)
241: 	}
242: 
243: 	var manifest AgentManifest
244: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &manifest); err != nil {
245: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal manifest: %w", err)
246: 	}
247: 
248: 	return &manifest, nil
249: }
250: 
251: // AgentManifestPath returns the path to the agent manifest file for a workspace.
252: func AgentManifestPath(workspacePath string) string {
253: 	return filepath.Join(workspacePath, AgentManifestFilename)
254: }
255: 
256: // ReadAgentManifestWithFallback reads the agent manifest from the workspace directory.
257: // Tries multiple sources in order:
258: // 1. OpenCode session metadata (if session_id exists and OpenCode is reachable)
259: // 2. AGENT_MANIFEST.json (if it exists)
260: // 3. Individual dotfiles (.beads_id, .tier, .spawn_time, .spawn_mode) for backward compatibility
261: // Always returns a non-nil manifest (fields may be empty if nothing is readable).
262: func ReadAgentManifestWithFallback(workspacePath string) *AgentManifest {
263: 	// Try OpenCode session metadata first (preferred source)
264: 	if manifest := readFromOpenCodeMetadata(workspacePath); manifest != nil {
265: 		return manifest
266: 	}
267: 
268: 	// Fallback 1: Read from AGENT_MANIFEST.json
269: 	manifest, err := ReadAgentManifest(workspacePath)
270: 	if err == nil {
271: 		return manifest
272: 	}
273: 
274: 	// Fallback 2: construct manifest from individual dotfiles
275: 	return readLegacyDotfiles(workspacePath)
276: }
277: 
278: // readFromOpenCodeMetadata tries to read agent manifest from OpenCode session metadata.
279: // Returns nil if session_id doesn't exist, OpenCode is unreachable, or session doesn't have metadata.
280: func readFromOpenCodeMetadata(workspacePath string) *AgentManifest {
281: 	// Read session ID from workspace
282: 	sessionID := ReadSessionID(workspacePath)
283: 	if sessionID == "" {
284: 		return nil // No session ID, can't query OpenCode
285: 	}
286: 
287: 	// Try to get session from OpenCode
288: 	client := opencode.NewClient(opencode.DefaultServerURL)
289: 	session, err := client.GetSession(sessionID)
290: 	if err != nil {
291: 		return nil // OpenCode unreachable or session not found
292: 	}
293: 
294: 	// If session has no metadata, return nil to fall back to other sources
295: 	if session.Metadata == nil || len(session.Metadata) == 0 {
296: 		return nil
297: 	}
298: 
299: 	// Construct manifest from session metadata
300: 	manifest := &AgentManifest{
301: 		WorkspaceName: filepath.Base(workspacePath),
302: 	}
303: 
304: 	if beadsID, ok := session.Metadata["beads_id"]; ok {
305: 		manifest.BeadsID = beadsID
306: 	}
307: 
308: 	if tier, ok := session.Metadata["tier"]; ok {
309: 		manifest.Tier = tier
310: 	} else {
311: 		manifest.Tier = TierFull // Default to "full" if not specified
312: 	}
313: 
314: 	if spawnMode, ok := session.Metadata["spawn_mode"]; ok {
315: 		manifest.SpawnMode = spawnMode
316: 	}
317: 
318: 	// Use session creation time as spawn time
319: 	if session.Time.Created > 0 {
320: 		manifest.SpawnTime = time.Unix(session.Time.Created, 0).Format(time.RFC3339)
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	return manifest
324: }
325: 
326: // readLegacyDotfiles constructs an AgentManifest from individual dotfiles.
327: // Used as fallback for pre-manifest workspaces.
328: func readLegacyDotfiles(workspacePath string) *AgentManifest {
329: 	manifest := &AgentManifest{
330: 		WorkspaceName: filepath.Base(workspacePath),
331: 	}
332: 
333: 	// Read .beads_id
334: 	if data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workspacePath, ".beads_id")); err == nil {
335: 		manifest.BeadsID = strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	// Read .tier (default to "full" if missing)
339: 	if data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workspacePath, TierFilename)); err == nil {
340: 		tier := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
341: 		if tier != "" {
342: 			manifest.Tier = tier
343: 		} else {
344: 			manifest.Tier = TierFull
345: 		}
346: 	} else {
347: 		manifest.Tier = TierFull
348: 	}
349: 
350: 	// Read .spawn_time (Unix nanos in dotfile -> RFC3339 in manifest)
351: 	if data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workspacePath, SpawnTimeFilename)); err == nil {
352: 		nanos, err := strconv.ParseInt(strings.TrimSpace(string(data)), 10, 64)
353: 		if err == nil {
354: 			manifest.SpawnTime = time.Unix(0, nanos).Format(time.RFC3339)
355: 		}
356: 	}
357: 
358: 	// Read .spawn_mode
359: 	if data, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workspacePath, ".spawn_mode")); err == nil {
360: 		manifest.SpawnMode = strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
361: 	}
362: 
363: 	return manifest
364: }
365: 
366: // ParseSpawnTime parses the manifest's SpawnTime string (RFC3339) to time.Time.
367: // Returns zero time if the field is empty or unparseable.
368: func (m *AgentManifest) ParseSpawnTime() time.Time {
369: 	if m.SpawnTime == "" {
370: 		return time.Time{}
371: 	}
372: 	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, m.SpawnTime)
373: 	if err != nil {
374: 		return time.Time{}
375: 	}
376: 	return t
377: }
378: 
379: // LookupManifestsByBeadsIDs scans workspace directories and returns manifests
380: // indexed by beads_id. Used by queryTrackedAgents for batch binding lookup.
381: // Only returns manifests whose BeadsID matches one of the provided IDs.
382: func LookupManifestsByBeadsIDs(projectDir string, beadsIDs []string) (map[string]*AgentManifest, error) {
383: 	if len(beadsIDs) == 0 {
384: 		return nil, nil
385: 	}
386: 
387: 	// Build lookup set for O(1) matching
388: 	idSet := make(map[string]bool, len(beadsIDs))
389: 	for _, id := range beadsIDs {
390: 		idSet[id] = true
391: 	}
392: 
393: 	workspaceRoot := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "workspace")
394: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(workspaceRoot)
395: 	if err != nil {
396: 		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
397: 			return nil, nil
398: 		}
399: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read workspace directory: %w", err)
400: 	}
401: 
402: 	result := make(map[string]*AgentManifest)
403: 	for _, entry := range entries {
404: 		if !entry.IsDir() {
405: 			continue
406: 		}
407: 		workspacePath := filepath.Join(workspaceRoot, entry.Name())
408: 		manifest, err := ReadAgentManifest(workspacePath)
409: 		if err != nil {
410: 			continue // Skip unreadable manifests
411: 		}
412: 		if manifest.BeadsID != "" && idSet[manifest.BeadsID] {
413: 			result[manifest.BeadsID] = manifest
414: 		}
415: 	}
416: 
417: 	return result, nil
418: }

<content>1: package backends
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"os"
6: 	"time"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/events"
9: 	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/spawn"
10: )
11: 
12: // FormatSessionTitle formats the session title to include beads ID for matching.
13: // Format: "workspace-name [beads-id]" (e.g., "og-debug-orch-status-23dec [orch-go-v4mw]")
14: // This allows extractBeadsIDFromTitle to find agents in orch status.
15: func FormatSessionTitle(workspaceName, beadsID string) string {
16: 	if beadsID == "" {
17: 		return workspaceName
18: 	}
19: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s [%s]", workspaceName, beadsID)
20: }
21: 
22: // AddGapAnalysisToEventData adds gap analysis information to an event data map.
23: // This enables tracking of context gaps for pattern analysis and dashboard surfacing.
24: func AddGapAnalysisToEventData(eventData map[string]interface{}, gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) {
25: 	if gapAnalysis == nil {
26: 		return
27: 	}
28: 
29: 	eventData["gap_has_gaps"] = gapAnalysis.HasGaps
30: 	eventData["gap_context_quality"] = gapAnalysis.ContextQuality
31: 
32: 	if gapAnalysis.HasGaps {
33: 		eventData["gap_should_warn"] = gapAnalysis.ShouldWarnAboutGaps()
34: 		eventData["gap_match_total"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches
35: 		eventData["gap_match_constraints"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount
36: 		eventData["gap_match_decisions"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.DecisionCount
37: 		eventData["gap_match_investigations"] = gapAnalysis.MatchStats.InvestigationCount
38: 
39: 		// Capture gap types for pattern analysis
40: 		var gapTypes []string
41: 		for _, gap := range gapAnalysis.Gaps {
42: 			gapTypes = append(gapTypes, string(gap.Type))
43: 		}
44: 		if len(gapTypes) > 0 {
45: 			eventData["gap_types"] = gapTypes
46: 		}
47: 	}
48: }
49: 
50: // AddUsageInfoToEventData adds usage information to an event data map.
51: // This enables tracking of rate limit patterns and account utilization at spawn time.
52: func AddUsageInfoToEventData(eventData map[string]interface{}, usageInfo *spawn.UsageInfo) {
53: 	if usageInfo == nil {
54: 		return
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	eventData["usage_5h_used"] = usageInfo.FiveHourUsed
58: 	eventData["usage_weekly_used"] = usageInfo.SevenDayUsed
59: 	if usageInfo.AccountEmail != "" {
60: 		eventData["usage_account"] = usageInfo.AccountEmail
61: 	}
62: 	if usageInfo.AutoSwitched {
63: 		eventData["usage_auto_switched"] = true
64: 		eventData["usage_switch_reason"] = usageInfo.SwitchReason
65: 	}
66: }
67: 
68: // FormatContextQualitySummary formats context quality for spawn summary output.
69: // Returns a formatted string with visual indicators for gap severity.
70: // This is the "prominent" surfacing that makes gaps hard to ignore.
71: func FormatContextQualitySummary(gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) string {
72: 	if gapAnalysis == nil {
73: 		return "not checked"
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	quality := gapAnalysis.ContextQuality
77: 
78: 	// Determine visual indicator and label based on quality level
79: 	var indicator, label string
80: 	switch {
81: 	case quality == 0:
82: 		indicator = "!"
83: 		label = "CRITICAL - No context"
84: 	case quality < 20:
85: 		indicator = "!"
86: 		label = "poor"
87: 	case quality < 40:
88: 		indicator = "!"
89: 		label = "limited"
90: 	case quality < 60:
91: 		indicator = "-"
92: 		label = "moderate"
93: 	case quality < 80:
94: 		indicator = "+"
95: 		label = "good"
96: 	default:
97: 		indicator = "+"
98: 		label = "excellent"
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	// Format the summary line
102: 	summary := fmt.Sprintf("%s %d/100 (%s)", indicator, quality, label)
103: 
104: 	// Add match breakdown for transparency
105: 	if gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches > 0 {
106: 		summary += fmt.Sprintf(" - %d matches", gapAnalysis.MatchStats.TotalMatches)
107: 		if gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount > 0 {
108: 			summary += fmt.Sprintf(" (%d constraints)", gapAnalysis.MatchStats.ConstraintCount)
109: 		}
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	return summary
113: }
114: 
115: // PrintSpawnSummaryWithGapWarning prints the spawn summary with prominent gap warnings.
116: // This ensures gaps are visible in the final output, not just during context gathering.
117: func PrintSpawnSummaryWithGapWarning(gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis) {
118: 	if gapAnalysis == nil || !gapAnalysis.ShouldWarnAboutGaps() {
119: 		return
120: 	}
121: 
122: 	// Print a prominent warning box for critical gaps
123: 	if gapAnalysis.HasCriticalGaps() || gapAnalysis.ContextQuality < 20 {
124: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n")
125: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n")
126: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "|  GAP WARNING: Agent spawned with limited context           |\n")
127: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n")
128: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "|  Agent may compensate by guessing patterns/conventions.    |\n")
129: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "|  Consider: kn decide / kn constrain / kb create            |\n")
130: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n")
131: 	}
132: }
133: 
134: // LogSpawnEvent logs the session.spawned event with common metadata.
135: func LogSpawnEvent(sessionID string, req *SpawnRequest, mode string, extraData map[string]interface{}) error {
136: 	logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
137: 
138: 	eventData := map[string]interface{}{
139: 		"skill":               req.SkillName,
140: 		"task":                req.Task,
141: 		"workspace":           req.Config.WorkspaceName,
142: 		"beads_id":            req.BeadsID,
143: 		"spawn_mode":          mode,
144: 		"no_track":            req.Config.NoTrack,
145: 		"skip_artifact_check": req.Config.SkipArtifactCheck,
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	// Add session_id for non-inline backends
149: 	if sessionID != "" {
150: 		eventData["session_id"] = sessionID
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	// Add model if present
154: 	if req.Config.Model != "" {
155: 		eventData["model"] = req.Config.Model
156: 	}
157: 
158: 	// Add MCP if present
159: 	if req.Config.MCP != "" {
160: 		eventData["mcp"] = req.Config.MCP
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	// Add extra data (e.g., retry_attempts, window info for tmux)
164: 	for k, v := range extraData {
165: 		eventData[k] = v
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	// Add gap analysis data
169: 	AddGapAnalysisToEventData(eventData, req.Config.GapAnalysis)
170: 
171: 	// Add usage info data
172: 	AddUsageInfoToEventData(eventData, req.Config.UsageInfo)
173: 
174: 	event := events.Event{
175: 		Type:      "session.spawned",
176: 		SessionID: sessionID,
177: 		Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
178: 		Data:      eventData,
179: 	}
180: 
181: 	return logger.Log(event)
182: }
183: 
184: // PrintSpawnSummary prints the common spawn summary output.
185: func PrintSpawnSummary(result *Result, req *SpawnRequest) {
186: 	// Print spawn summary with prominent gap warning if needed
187: 	PrintSpawnSummaryWithGapWarning(req.Config.GapAnalysis)
188: 
189: 	switch result.SpawnMode {
190: 	case "inline":
191: 		fmt.Printf("Spawned agent:\n")
192: 		fmt.Printf("  Session ID: %s\n", result.SessionID)
193: 		fmt.Printf("  Workspace:  %s\n", req.Config.WorkspaceName)
194: 		fmt.Printf("  Beads ID:   %s\n", req.BeadsID)
195: 		fmt.Printf("  Context:    %s\n", FormatContextQualitySummary(req.Config.GapAnalysis))
196: 
197: 	case "headless":
198: 		fmt.Printf("Spawned agent (headless):\n")
199: 		fmt.Printf("  Session ID: %s\n", result.SessionID)
200: 		fmt.Printf("  Workspace:  %s\n", req.Config.WorkspaceName)
201: 		fmt.Printf("  Beads ID:   %s\n", req.BeadsID)
202: 		fmt.Printf("  Model:      %s\n", req.Config.Model)
203: 		if req.Config.MCP != "" {
204: 			fmt.Printf("  MCP:        %s\n", req.Config.MCP)

207: 			fmt.Printf("  Tracking:   disabled (--no-track)\n")
208: 		}
209: 		fmt.Printf("  Context:    %s\n", FormatContextQualitySummary(req.Config.GapAnalysis))
210: 
211: 	case "tmux":
212: 		if result.TmuxInfo != nil {
213: 			fmt.Printf("Spawned agent in tmux:\n")
