state.output:
<path>/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/pkg/spawn/backends/headless.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package backends
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/opencode"
9: 	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/spawn"
10: )
11: 
12: // HeadlessBackend spawns the agent via the OpenCode HTTP API without a TUI.
13: // This is useful for automation and daemon-driven spawns.
14: // Uses HTTP API with x-opencode-directory header to properly set the session's
15: // working directory for cross-project spawns.
16: // Includes retry logic for transient network failures.
17: type HeadlessBackend struct{}
18: 
19: // Name returns the backend name.
20: func (b *HeadlessBackend) Name() string {
21: 	return "headless"
22: }
23: 
24: // headlessSpawnResult contains the result of starting a headless session.
25: type headlessSpawnResult struct {
26: 	SessionID string
27: }
28: 
29: // Spawn creates a new agent session in headless mode.
30: func (b *HeadlessBackend) Spawn(ctx context.Context, req *SpawnRequest) (*Result, error) {
31: 	client := opencode.NewClient(req.ServerURL)
32: 
33: 	// Format title with beads ID so orch status can match sessions
34: 	sessionTitle := FormatSessionTitle(req.Config.WorkspaceName, req.BeadsID)
35: 
36: 	// Use retry logic for transient failures (network issues, server temporarily unavailable)
37: 	retryCfg := spawn.DefaultRetryConfig()
38: 	result, retryResult := spawn.Retry(retryCfg, func() (*headlessSpawnResult, error) {
39: 		return startHeadlessSession(client, req.ServerURL, sessionTitle, req.MinimalPrompt, req.Config)
40: 	})
41: 
42: 	if retryResult.LastErr != nil {
43: 		// Wrap the error with user-friendly message and recovery guidance
44: 		spawnErr := spawn.WrapSpawnError(retryResult.LastErr, "Headless spawn failed")
45: 		if retryResult.Attempts > 1 {
46: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Spawn failed after %d attempts\n", retryResult.Attempts)
47: 		}
48: 		// Print formatted error with recovery guidance
49: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n%s\n", spawn.FormatSpawnError(spawnErr))
50: 		return nil, spawnErr
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	if retryResult.Attempts > 1 {
54: 		fmt.Printf("Spawn succeeded after %d attempts\n", retryResult.Attempts)
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	sessionID := result.SessionID
58: 
59: 	// Write session ID to workspace file for later lookups
60: 	if err := spawn.WriteSessionID(req.Config.WorkspacePath(), sessionID); err != nil {
61: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to write session ID: %v\n", err)
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	// Orchestrator sessions use workspace artifacts for tracking
65: 	// Build extra event data for retries
66: 	var extraData map[string]interface{}
67: 	if retryResult.Attempts > 1 {
68: 		extraData = map[string]interface{}{
69: 			"retry_attempts": retryResult.Attempts,
70: 		}
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	// Log the session creation
74: 	if err := LogSpawnEvent(sessionID, req, "headless", extraData); err != nil {
75: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log event: %v\n", err)
76: 	}
77: 
78: 	return &Result{
79: 		SessionID:     sessionID,
80: 		SpawnMode:     "headless",
81: 		RetryAttempts: retryResult.Attempts,
82: 	}, nil
83: }
84: 
85: // startHeadlessSession creates an OpenCode session via HTTP API and sends the initial prompt.
86: // Uses HTTP API instead of CLI subprocess to properly set the session's working directory
87: // via x-opencode-directory header. This fixes cross-project spawns where --workdir differs
88: // from the orchestrator's CWD.
89: // Model selection is handled per-message by SendMessageInDirectory (providerID/modelID format).
90: func startHeadlessSession(client *opencode.Client, serverURL, sessionTitle, minimalPrompt string, cfg *spawn.Config) (*headlessSpawnResult, error) {
91: 	// Step 1: Create session via HTTP API with correct directory
92: 	// CreateSession passes x-opencode-directory header so the server uses the target project dir
93: 	metadata := map[string]string{
94: 		"beads_id":       cfg.BeadsID,
95: 		"workspace_path": cfg.WorkspacePath(),
96: 		"tier":           cfg.Tier,
97: 		"spawn_mode":     "headless",
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	// Calculate TTL based on session type
101: 	// Worker sessions: 4 hours (14400 seconds)
102: 	// Orchestrator sessions: 0 (no expiration)
103: 	timeTTL := 4 * 60 * 60 // 4 hours in seconds
104: 	if cfg.IsOrchestrator {
105: 		timeTTL = 0 // Orchestrator sessions persist until manually cleaned
106: 	}
107: 
108: 	session, err := client.CreateSession(sessionTitle, cfg.ProjectDir, cfg.Model, metadata, timeTTL)
109: 	if err != nil {
110: 		return nil, spawn.WrapSpawnError(err, "Failed to create session via API")
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	// Step 2: Send the initial prompt with model selection and directory context
114: 	// The directory header ensures the server resolves the correct project context
115: 	if err := client.SendMessageInDirectory(session.ID, minimalPrompt, cfg.Model, cfg.ProjectDir); err != nil {
116: 		return nil, spawn.WrapSpawnError(err, "Failed to send prompt to session")
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	return &headlessSpawnResult{
120: 		SessionID: session.ID,
121: 	}, nil
122: }

part.text:
**Designing new sessions commands and data types**

I'm planning to implement new data types for the /api/sessions endpoint to handle metadata like "role" and "no_track," and to create a new root CLI command "sessions" focused on untracked sessions. I'll keep existing session-related commands but introduce subcommands under "orch sessions" for live/untracked and history views to align with requirements.

**Refactoring CLI sessions commands for separation**

I'm consolidating untracked sessions under a new `orch sessions` command while migrating existing session history commands to a new `orch session-history` command to maintain two-lane separation without overlap. The plan includes categorizing sessions by metadata (role=orchestrator, no_track, ad-hoc) with clear priority, preserving old functionality under the new command, and designing minimal CLI flags plus a new /api/sessions endpoint for untracked sessions with structured JSON output.

**Aligning untracked session status logic**

I'm adopting the same active/idle/dead thresholds from agents logic to classify untracked sessions' status based on last update time and processing state, while simplifying the output to include key metadata fields and timing for the new CLI and API responses. I'll implement separate flag variables and commands in separate files to avoid naming conflicts and prepare for the new /api/sessions endpoint with potential future filters like project or category.

10: // parseSinceParam parses the ?since= query parameter into a time.Duration.
11: // Supported formats:
12: //   - 12h, 24h, 48h (hours)
13: //   - 7d (days)
14: //   - all (returns 0, meaning no filtering)
15: //
16: // Default: 12h if not specified or invalid.
17: func parseSinceParam(r *http.Request) time.Duration {
18: 	since := r.URL.Query().Get("since")
19: 	if since == "" || since == "all" {
20: 		// "all" means no time filtering - return 0
21: 		if since == "all" {
22: 			return 0
23: 		}
24: 		// Default to 12h if not specified
25: 		return 12 * time.Hour
26: 	}
27: 
28: 	// Try parsing as hours (e.g., "12h", "24h")
29: 	if strings.HasSuffix(since, "h") {
30: 		hours, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSuffix(since, "h"))
31: 		if err == nil && hours > 0 {
32: 			return time.Duration(hours) * time.Hour
33: 		}
34: 	}
35: 
36: 	// Try parsing as days (e.g., "7d")
37: 	if strings.HasSuffix(since, "d") {
38: 		days, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSuffix(since, "d"))
39: 		if err == nil && days > 0 {
40: 			return time.Duration(days) * 24 * time.Hour
41: 		}
42: 	}
43: 
44: 	// Default fallback
45: 	return 12 * time.Hour
46: }
47: 
48: // parseProjectFilter parses the ?project= query parameter.
49: // Supports comma-separated values for multi-project filtering (e.g., "?project=orch-go,orch-cli,beads").
50: // Returns empty slice if not specified (meaning no project filtering).
51: func parseProjectFilter(r *http.Request) []string {
52: 	param := r.URL.Query().Get("project")
53: 	if param == "" {
54: 		return nil
55: 	}
56: 	// Split on comma and trim whitespace from each project name
57: 	projects := strings.Split(param, ",")
58: 	result := make([]string, 0, len(projects))
59: 	for _, p := range projects {
60: 		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(p)
61: 		if trimmed != "" {
62: 			result = append(result, trimmed)
63: 		}
64: 	}
65: 	return result
66: }
67: 
68: // filterByTime returns true if the timestamp is within the since duration.
69: // If sinceDuration is 0, returns true (no filtering).
70: func filterByTime(timestamp time.Time, sinceDuration time.Duration) bool {
71: 	if sinceDuration == 0 {
72: 		return true // "all" mode - no time filtering
73: 	}
74: 	return time.Since(timestamp) <= sinceDuration
75: }
76: 
77: // filterByProject returns true if the projectDir matches ANY of the filters.
78: // If filters is empty, returns true (no filtering).
79: // Matches on:
80: //   - Full path match
81: //   - Project name (last path segment) match
82: func filterByProject(projectDir string, filters []string) bool {
83: 	if len(filters) == 0 {
84: 		return true // No filtering
85: 	}
86: 	if projectDir == "" {
87: 		return false // No project_dir to match against
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	// Check if projectDir matches ANY of the filters
91: 	for _, filter := range filters {
92: 		// Full path match
93: 		if projectDir == filter {
94: 			return true
95: 		}
96: 
97: 		// Extract project name from path and match
98: 		// e.g., "/Users/dylan/orch-go" -> "orch-go"
99: 		projectName := extractProjectName(projectDir)
100: 		filterName := extractProjectName(filter)
101: 		if projectName == filterName {
102: 			return true
103: 		}
104: 	}
105: 
106: 	return false
107: }
108: 
109: // extractProjectName extracts the last path segment from a directory path.
110: func extractProjectName(dir string) string {
111: 	if dir == "" {
112: 		return ""
113: 	}
114: 	// Handle trailing slash
115: 	dir = strings.TrimSuffix(dir, "/")
116: 	// Get last segment
117: 	if idx := strings.LastIndex(dir, "/"); idx != -1 {
118: 		return dir[idx+1:]
119: 	}
120: 	return dir
121: }

part.text:
**Planning CLI sessions command update**

I'm renaming the existing sessions history command and adding a new sessions command to list untracked sessions with filters like limit and project, parsing metadata fields for classification including role and no_track flags.

**Detailing untracked session classification and CLI/API design**

I've defined strict metadata-based rules for categorizing untracked sessions to avoid overlapping with tracked ones, planned helper functions and shared code for CLI and API, and scoped CLI flags and API response fields to provide filtered, sorted, and categorized outputs with clear status and metadata included. Next, I'll verify the project location per deliverables.

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/cmd/orch/sessions_untracked.go
+package main
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"sort"
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/opencode"
+	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
+)
+
+var (
+	untrackedSessionsJSON    bool
+	untrackedSessionsLimit   int
+	untrackedSessionsProject string
+)
+
+var sessionsCmd = &cobra.Command{
+	Use:   "sessions",
+	Short: "List untracked OpenCode sessions (orchestrator/ad-hoc/no-track)",
+	Long: `List untracked OpenCode sessions.
+
+This command shows sessions that do NOT map to beads-tracked work items:
+- orchestrator sessions (role=orchestrator)
+- ad-hoc sessions (no beads_id)
+- --no-track sessions (explicit opt-out)
+
+Two-lane split:
+  orch status   = tracked work only
+  orch sessions = untracked sessions only
+
+Examples:
+  orch sessions
+  orch sessions --limit 50
+  orch sessions --project orch-go
+  orch sessions --json`,
+	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+		return runUntrackedSessions(serverURL)
+	},
+}
+
+func init() {
+	sessionsCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&untrackedSessionsJSON, "json", false, "Output as JSON")
+	sessionsCmd.Flags().IntVar(&untrackedSessionsLimit, "limit", 25, "Maximum number of sessions to show")
+	sessionsCmd.Flags().StringVar(&untrackedSessionsProject, "project", "", "Filter by project (name or path)")
+
+	rootCmd.AddCommand(sessionsCmd)
+}
+
+type UntrackedSessionOutput struct {
+	ID            string `json:"id"`
+	Title         string `json:"title,omitempty"`
+	Category      string `json:"category"`
+	Role          string `json:"role,omitempty"`
+	BeadsID       string `json:"beads_id,omitempty"`
+	Tier          string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
+	SpawnMode     string `json:"spawn_mode,omitempty"`
+	Skill         string `json:"skill,omitempty"`
+	Model         string `json:"model,omitempty"`
+	WorkspacePath string `json:"workspace_path,omitempty"`
+	ProjectDir    string `json:"project_dir,omitempty"`
+	CreatedAt     string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
+	UpdatedAt     string `json:"updated_at,omitempty"`
+}
+
+func runUntrackedSessions(serverURL string) error {
+	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
+	projectDir, _ := os.Getwd()
+
+	untracked, err := listUntrackedSessions(client, projectDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to list sessions: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	filters := parseProjectFilters(untrackedSessionsProject)
+	filtered := make([]UntrackedSessionOutput, 0, len(untracked))
+	for _, entry := range untracked {
+		if len(filters) > 0 && !filterByProject(entry.Session.Directory, filters) {
+			continue
+		}
+		filtered = append(filtered, entry.toOutput())
+	}
+
+	sort.Slice(filtered, func(i, j int) bool {
+		return filtered[i].UpdatedAt > filtered[j].UpdatedAt
+	})
+
+	if untrackedSessionsLimit > 0 && len(filtered) > untrackedSessionsLimit {
+		filtered = filtered[:untrackedSessionsLimit]
+	}
+
+	if untrackedSessionsJSON {
+		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(filtered, "", "  ")
+		if err != nil {
+			return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal JSON: %w", err)
+		}
+		fmt.Println(string(data))
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	if len(filtered) == 0 {
+		fmt.Println("No untracked sessions found")
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	fmt.Printf("Found %d untracked sessions:\n\n", len(filtered))
+	for _, session := range filtered {
+		updated := session.UpdatedAt
+		if updated == "" {
+			updated = "unknown"
+		}
+		fmt.Printf("%-22s  %s\n", session.ID, truncate(session.Title, 70))
+		fmt.Printf("  Category: %s | Updated: %s\n", session.Category, updated)
+		if session.ProjectDir != "" {
+			fmt.Printf("  Project:  %s\n", session.ProjectDir)
+		}
+		if session.Role != "" || session.Skill != "" || session.Tier != "" || session.SpawnMode != "" {
+			fmt.Printf("  Meta:     role=%s skill=%s tier=%s mode=%s\n",
+				emptyFallback(session.Role, "-"),
+				emptyFallback(session.Skill, "-"),
+				emptyFallback(session.Tier, "-"),
+				emptyFallback(session.SpawnMode, "-"))
+		}
+		fmt.Println()
+	}
+
+	return nil
+}
+
+func parseProjectFilters(value string) []string {
+	if value == "" {
+		return nil
+	}
+	parts := strings.Split(value, ",")
+	result := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
+	for _, part := range parts {
+		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(part)
+		if trimmed != "" {
+			result = append(result, trimmed)
+		}
+	}
+	return result
+}
+
+func emptyFallback(value, fallback string) string {
+	if value == "" {
+		return fallback
+	}
+	return value
+}
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/cmd/orch/serve_sessions.go
+package main
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"log"
+	"net/http"
+	"sort"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/opencode"
+)
+
+// SessionAPIResponse is the JSON structure returned by /api/sessions.
+type SessionAPIResponse struct {
+	ID            string `json:"id"`
+	Title         string `json:"title,omitempty"`
+	Category      string `json:"category"`
+	Role          string `json:"role,omitempty"`
+	BeadsID       string `json:"beads_id,omitempty"`
+	Tier          string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
+	SpawnMode     string `json:"spawn_mode,omitempty"`
+	Skill         string `json:"skill,omitempty"`
+	Model         string `json:"model,omitempty"`
+	WorkspacePath string `json:"workspace_path,omitempty"`
+	ProjectDir    string `json:"project_dir,omitempty"`
+	Status        string `json:"status,omitempty"`
+	IsProcessing  bool   `json:"is_processing,omitempty"`
+	CreatedAt     string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
+	UpdatedAt     string `json:"updated_at,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// handleSessions returns JSON list of untracked OpenCode sessions.
+// Query parameters:
+//   - since: Time filter (12h, 24h, 48h, 7d, all). Default: 12h
+//   - project: Project filter (full path or project name). Default: none (all projects)
+func handleSessions(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+	if r.Method != http.MethodGet {
+		http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
+		return
+	}
+
+	sinceDuration := parseSinceParam(r)
+	projectFilterParam := parseProjectFilter(r)
+
+	projectDir := sourceDir
+	if projectDir == "" || projectDir == "unknown" {
+		projectDir, _ = os.Getwd()
+	}
+
+	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
+
+	untracked, err := listUntrackedSessions(client, projectDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		log.Printf("Warning: failed to list sessions: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	sessionStatusMap := make(map[string]opencode.SessionStatusInfo)
+	if status, err := client.GetAllSessionStatus(); err != nil {
+		log.Printf("Warning: failed to fetch session status: %v", err)
+	} else {
+		sessionStatusMap = status
+	}
+
+	now := time.Now()
+	responses := make([]SessionAPIResponse, 0, len(untracked))
+	for _, entry := range untracked {
+		updatedAt := time.Unix(entry.Session.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
+		createdAt := time.Unix(entry.Session.Time.Created/1000, 0)
+		sessionTime := updatedAt
+		if sessionTime.IsZero() {
+			sessionTime = createdAt
+		}
+
+		if !filterByTime(sessionTime, sinceDuration) {
+			continue
+		}
+		if len(projectFilterParam) > 0 && !filterByProject(entry.Session.Directory, projectFilterParam) {
+			continue
+		}
+
+		status := "idle"
+		isProcessing := false
+		if statusInfo, ok := sessionStatusMap[entry.Session.ID]; ok {
+			isProcessing = statusInfo.IsBusy() || statusInfo.IsRetrying()
+			if isProcessing {
+				status = "active"
+			}
+		}
+
+		responses = append(responses, SessionAPIResponse{
+			ID:            entry.Session.ID,
+			Title:         entry.Session.Title,
+			Category:      entry.Category,
+			Role:          entry.Role,
+			BeadsID:       entry.BeadsID,
+			Tier:          entry.Tier,
+			SpawnMode:     entry.SpawnMode,
+			Skill:         entry.Skill,
+			Model:         entry.Model,
+			WorkspacePath: entry.WorkspacePath,
+			ProjectDir:    entry.Session.Directory,
+			Status:        status,
+			IsProcessing:  isProcessing,
+			CreatedAt:     formatSessionTime(entry.Session.Time.Created),
+			UpdatedAt:     formatSessionTime(entry.Session.Time.Updated),
+		})
+	}
+
+	sort.Slice(responses, func(i, j int) bool {
+		return responses[i].UpdatedAt > responses[j].UpdatedAt
+	})
+
+	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
+	if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(responses); err != nil {
+		http.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf("Failed to encode sessions: %v", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)
+		return
+	}
+}
*** End Patch

 {"id":"orch-go-1023","title":"Dashboard work-graph usability improvements: visibility, sorting, and clarity","description":"The work-graph Issues view is structurally working (dependency trees, Ready to Complete, independent issues) but has several usability gaps that make it hard to orient at a glance:

**1. In-progress issues don't visually stand out**
The play icon + green subline is too subtle. P1 in-progress bugs should pop — left border accent, background highlight, or similar treatment to the Ready to Complete section.

**2. P1s buried in INDEPENDENT ISSUES**
Two P1 bugs are mixed in with 25 P2s. Priority should sort within sections — P1s at top, not mixed by creation order.

**3. 'No active agent linked' is noisy and negative**
Shown on every in-progress issue (partly due to orch-go-1022 tmux bug). Even when fixed, the subline should show agent phase + runtime when linked, and just 'unassigned' or nothing when not — not an alarming message.

**4. Ready to Complete shows stale/already-closed entries**
980.1 (already closed) and 990 (failed Codex attempt with stale Phase: Complete) still appear. Section should only show issues that are actually open + Phase: Complete.

**5. 27 independent issues is overwhelming**
Most are backlog. Default should show active work (in-progress + recently created) and collapse/paginate the rest. Or separate 'Active' vs 'Backlog' sections.

**6. Completed agents section competes with Ready to Complete**
The completed agents banner (token counts, timing) is useful but visually competes with the more actionable Ready to Complete section. Consider collapsing by default or moving to Completed tab.

**7. Dependency chain header uses issue title as section name**
Chain header shows the title of issue 980.4 which reads oddly as a group name.  {"id":"orch-go-1025","title":"Design and implement orch rework command for sending agents back with specific completion review feedback","description":"Completion review regularly finds gaps in agent work (e.g. xu8l shipped without removing flag definitions, sg4k sort has no effect). Currently the workaround is spawning a fresh agent with REWORK prefix in the prompt. A dedicated orch rework <beads-id> 'feedback' command would: (1) reopen the issue, (2) inject the rework feedback as context alongside the original SYNTHESIS.md, (3) spawn a new agent against the same issue with both the original task and the rework feedback, (4) track it as a rework (not a fresh attempt) for completion metrics.  {"id":"orch-go-1081","title":"ADR: Two-lane agent discovery with atomic spawn, reason codes, and regression guardrails","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-18T23:15:57.995057-08:00","created_by":"dylanconlin","updated_at":"2026-02-18T23:27:19.528924-08:00","closed_at":"2026-02-18T23:27:19.528924-08:00","labels":["area:dashboard","area:spawn","triage:review","daemon:ready-review"],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"orch-go-1081","author":"dylanconlin","text":"FRAME: This is the architectural decision that breaks the 5-iteration cache/remove cycle in agent observability. Dylan spent 6 weeks (Dec 21 - Feb 18) fighting ghost agents, phantom status, and silent enrichment failures caused by multi-source reconciliation. Five local state layers (registry.json, sessions.json, state.db, workspace cache, multi-source reconciliation) were each built to solve slow/incomplete queries and each drifted from reality.

The current beads-first solution (Feb 18) correctly inverts the query direction but has two unresolved pressure points: --no-track agents are invisible, and orchestrator sessions don't fit the beads work-item model. These are the exact gaps that triggered cache-building in January. Without addressing them, the cycle restarts.

## Deliverable

Write an ADR at .kb/decisions/2026-02-18-two-lane-agent-discovery.md with an acceptance test matrix. The ADR must cover all 6 design points below.

## Design (from Codex analysis, validated by orchestrator)

### 1. Lock domain boundaries (enforceable, not just preferred)

- beads = lifecycle for tracked work (exists/done)
- workspace manifest = binding (beads_id <-> session_id <-> project_dir)  
- OpenCode = liveness only
- tmux = presentation only
- No other persisted lifecycle state allowed

### 2. Two-lane split: tracked work vs untracked sessions

- orch status / dashboard /api/agents: tracked agents only (must have beads_id)
- orch sessions / /api/sessions: orchestrator, ad-hoc, --no-track sessions
- Stop trying to merge these into one truth view
- This resolves the Jan 5 semantic mismatch ('orchestrators are not issues') that killed beads-first the first time

### 3. Atomic spawn (all-or-nothing)

On spawn, require all 3 writes succeed or spawn fails:
  1. beads issue/tag or beads linkage
  2. workspace manifest with beads_id, session_id, project_dir
  3. spawn evidence marker
No partial state. A half-spawned agent is worse than a failed spawn.

Tradeoff to document: beads availability becomes a hard dependency. If beads is down, spawn fails. This is intentional — partial state caused the 238-dead-agents bug (orch-go-1074).

### 4. Single-pass query with reason codes

issues := beads.ListInProgress(tag='orch:agent')
bindings := workspace.LookupByBeadsIDs(issues.IDs)
live := opencode.BatchLiveness(bindings.SessionIDs)
return JoinWithReasonCodes(issues, bindings, live)

Never return silent empty metadata. Return explicit reason codes (missing_binding, missing_session, session_dead, etc.). Every previous debugging session was caused by silent enrichment failures.

### 5. Performance without drift

- Allow ONLY in-memory TTL cache (seconds), process-local, non-authoritative
- No disk-backed projection cache for lifecycle data
- Dashboard server (long-lived process) benefits from in-memory cache
- CLI commands (short-lived process) hit full query cost each invocation — acceptable
- Target: dashboard <500ms, CLI <2s (currently 4s)

### 6. Regression guardrails

- Contract tests: spawn -> visible, complete -> gone, restart resilience, cross-project --workdir, concurrent spawns
- Architecture lint rule: forbid new persistent lifecycle store packages (pkg/state/, pkg/registry/, pkg/cache/ etc)
- These are structural gates, not reminders. Agents ignore reminders under pressure.

## Acceptance Test Matrix (include in ADR)

| Scenario | Lane | Expected | Reason code if missing |
|----------|------|----------|----------------------|
| Tracked agent spawned | orch status | visible with full metadata | — |
| Tracked agent completed | orch status | gone (issue closed) | — |
| --no-track agent spawned | orch sessions | visible | — |
| --no-track agent spawned | orch status | NOT visible (by design) | — |
| Orchestrator session | orch sessions | visible | — |
| Beads down during spawn | — | spawn fails with error | — |
| OpenCode down | orch status | agents shown, status=unknown | missing_session |
| Workspace missing | orch status | agent shown, metadata partial | missing_binding |
| Cross-project --workdir | orch status | correct project_dir | — |
| Concurrent spawns (5x) | orch status | all 5 visible, no duplicates | — |
| Server restart | orch status | agents survive, no ghosts | — |
| New pkg/state/ file added | CI | lint failure, blocked | — |

## Complementary work (separate track, not in this ADR)

orch-go-1078/1079/1080: OpenCode fork session metadata API. Makes the authoritative source richer so the workspace cross-reference step eventually becomes unnecessary. The ADR should note this as a future simplification but not depend on it.

## References

- .kb/investigations/2025-12-21-synthesis-registry-evolution-and-orch-identity.md (full cycle history)
- .kb/investigations/2026-02-18-design-agent-observability-rethink.md (beads-first design)
- .kb/decisions/2026-02-14-lifecycle-ownership-own-accept-build.md (Own/Accept/Build)
- .kb/decisions/2026-01-12-registry-is-spawn-cache.md (the decision that defended the registry)
- orch-go-1074 (238 dead agents from partial state)
- orch-go-1058 (strategic question that led to beads-first)","created_at":"2026-02-18T23:16:28.081988-08:00"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"orch-go-1081","author":"dylanconlin","text":"FRAME: Write ADR for two-lane agent discovery architecture with atomic spawn, reason codes, and regression guardrails. Full design spec and acceptance test matrix are in the beads issue comments — read orch-go-1081 for complete requirements. Deliverable: .kb/decisions/2026-02-18-two-lane-agent-discovery.md","created_at":"2026-02-18T23:21:27.717158-08:00"},{"id":3,"issue_id":"orch-go-1081","author":"dylanconlin","text":"Phase: Planning - Reading beads issue comments for full design spec and acceptance test matrix","created_at":"2026-02-18T23:21:34.214965-08:00"},{"id":4,"issue_id":"orch-go-1081","author":"dylanconlin","text":"Phase: Implementing - Writing ADR with full design spec and acceptance test matrix","created_at":"2026-02-18T23:22:02.739121-08:00"},{"id":5,"issue_id":"orch-go-1081","author":"dylanconlin","text":"Phase: Complete - ADR written at .kb/decisions/2026-02-18-two-lane-agent-discovery.md with all 6 design points (domain boundaries, two-lane split, atomic spawn, reason codes, performance constraints, regression guardrails) and 12-scenario acceptance test matrix. No discovered work.","created_at":"2026-02-18T23:24:12.907433-08:00"},{"id":6,"issue_id":"orch-go-1081","author":"dylanconlin","text":"EXPLAIN-BACK:
ADR covers all 6 design points: domain boundaries, two-lane split, atomic spawn, reason codes, performance constraints, and regression guardrails. Acceptance test matrix has 12 scenarios. Correctly supersedes the two old registry decisions (Jan 12, Jan 14). This is the architectural spec that breaks the 5-iteration cache cycle.","created_at":"2026-02-18T23:27:19.465941-08:00"}]}

-{"id":"orch-go-1083","title":"Architect: atomic spawn with workspace manifest and orch:agent tagging","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-19T08:38:10.212695-08:00","created_by":"dylanconlin","updated_at":"2026-02-19T09:07:20.052273-08:00","labels":["area:spawn","area:observability","triage:review","daemon:ready-review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"orch-go-1083","depends_on_id":"orch-go-1089","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"orch-go-1083","author":"dylanconlin","text":"FRAME: This is the foundation of the two-lane agent discovery architecture. Current spawn is fire-and-forget — partial state caused the 238-dead-agents bug (1074). This refactor makes spawn atomic: all 3 writes succeed or spawn fails entirely.

DESIGN (from ADR .kb/decisions/2026-02-18-two-lane-agent-discovery.md):

1. ATOMIC SPAWN: On spawn, require all writes succeed or rollback:
   a) Create/tag beads issue with orch:agent
   b) Write workspace manifest (beads_id, session_id, project_dir, tier, spawn_mode)
   c) Create OpenCode session with metadata (beads_id, workspace_path, tier)
   d) Write spawn evidence marker
   If any fail, rollback all previous steps. No partial state.

2. WORKSPACE MANIFEST: Structured binding file in .orch/workspace/{name}/manifest.json
   - Must support batch lookup by beads_id (workspace.LookupByBeadsIDs)
   - Contains: beads_id, session_id, project_dir, spawn_time, tier, spawn_mode

3. SESSION METADATA: At spawn, store in OpenCode session.metadata:
   beads_id, workspace_path, tier (API verified in 1079 probe)

4. orch:agent TAG: All tracked agents get this beads tag for efficient listing

ARCHITECT FIRST: spawn_cmd.go is likely a hotspot. Check line count. If >1,500 lines, extract spawn pipeline before adding atomic spawn. See .kb/guides/code-extraction-patterns.md.

REFERENCES: ADR sections 'Atomic Spawn' and 'Domain Boundaries'. Pseudocode in ADR.","created_at":"2026-02-19T08:38:44.158214-08:00"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"orch-go-1083","author":"dylanconlin","text":"FRAME: Architect: atomic spawn with workspace manifest and orch:agent tagging. Current spawn is fire-and-forget — partial state caused the 238-dead-agents bug (1074). This refactor makes spawn atomic: all 3 writes succeed or spawn fails entirely.

DESIGN (from ADR .kb/decisions/2026-02-18-two-lane-agent-discovery.md):

1. ATOMIC SPAWN: On spawn, require all writes succeed or rollback:
   a) Create/tag beads issue with orch:agent
   b) Write workspace manifest (beads_id, session_id, project_dir, tier, spawn_mode)
   c) Create OpenCode session with metadata (beads_id, workspace_path, tier)
   d) Write spawn evidence marker
   If any fail, rollback all previous steps. No partial state.

2. WORKSPACE MANIFEST: Structured binding file in .orch/workspace/{name}/manifest.json
   - Must support batch lookup by beads_id (workspace.LookupByBeadsIDs)
   - Contains: beads_id, session_id, project_dir, spawn_time, tier, spawn_mode

3. SESSION METADATA: At spawn, store in OpenCode session.metadata:
   beads_id, workspace_path, tier (API verified in 1079 probe)

4. orch:agent TAG: All tracked agents get this beads tag for efficient listing

ARCHITECT FIRST: spawn_cmd.go is likely a hotspot. Check line count. If >1,500 lines, extract spawn pipeline before adding atomic spawn. See .kb/guides/code-extraction-patterns.md.

REFERENCES: ADR sections 'Atomic Spawn' and 'Domain Boundaries'. Pseudocode in ADR.","created_at":"2026-02-19T08:38:44.158214-08:00"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"orch-go-1083","author":"dylanconlin","text":"FRAME: Architect: atomic spawn with workspace manifest and orch:agent tagging. -{"id":"orch-go-1085","title":"Implement single-pass query engine with reason codes (queryTrackedAgents)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-19T08:38:16.024063-08:00","created_by":"dylanconlin","updated_at":"2026-02-19T08:38:16.024063-08:00","labels":["area:observability","triage:review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"orch-go-1085","depends_on_id":"orch-go-1083","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"orch-go-1085","author":"dylanconlin","text":"FRAME: This is the query engine that replaces ad-hoc cross-referencing with a single deterministic path. Every previous debugging session was caused by silent enrichment failures — this function makes failure modes visible via reason codes.

DESIGN (from ADR section 'Single-Pass Query with Reason Codes'):

AgentStatus struct:
  BeadsID, SessionID, Status, Phase, ProjectDir
  MissingBinding bool  // workspace manifest not found
  MissingSession bool  // OpenCode session not found
  SessionDead bool     // session idle/errored
  MissingPhase bool    // no Phase comment in beads
  Reason string        // human-readable

queryTrackedAgents(projectDirs []string) ([]AgentStatus, error):
  1. beads.ListByTag('orch:agent', status='in_progress')
  2. workspace.LookupByBeadsIDs(issueIDs)
  3. opencode.BatchLiveness(sessionIDs)
  4. JoinWithReasonCodes(issues, bindings, liveness)

DEGRADED MODES:
  - OpenCode down → agents shown with status=unknown
  - Workspace missing → agent shown with reason=missing_binding
  - Never return silent empty metadata

DEPENDS ON: 1083 (atomic spawn creates the workspace manifests this queries)
REFERENCE: ADR pseudocode for queryTrackedAgents","created_at":"2026-02-19T08:39:00.116383-08:00"}]}

diff --git a/cmd/orch/serve_sessions.go b/cmd/orch/serve_sessions.go
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..148d04433
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cmd/orch/serve_sessions.go
@@ -0,0 +1,119 @@
+package main
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"log"
+	"net/http"
+	"os"
+	"sort"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/opencode"
+)
+
+// SessionAPIResponse is the JSON structure returned by /api/sessions.
+type SessionAPIResponse struct {
+	ID            string `json:"id"`
+	Title         string `json:"title,omitempty"`
+	Category      string `json:"category"`
+	Role          string `json:"role,omitempty"`
+	BeadsID       string `json:"beads_id,omitempty"`
+	Tier          string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
+	SpawnMode     string `json:"spawn_mode,omitempty"`
+	Skill         string `json:"skill,omitempty"`
+	Model         string `json:"model,omitempty"`
+	WorkspacePath string `json:"workspace_path,omitempty"`
+	ProjectDir    string `json:"project_dir,omitempty"`
+	Status        string `json:"status,omitempty"`
+	IsProcessing  bool   `json:"is_processing,omitempty"`
+	CreatedAt     string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
+	UpdatedAt     string `json:"updated_at,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// handleSessions returns JSON list of untracked OpenCode sessions.
+// Query parameters:
+//   - since: Time filter (12h, 24h, 48h, 7d, all). Default: 12h
+//   - project: Project filter (full path or project name). Default: none (all projects)
+func handleSessions(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
+	if r.Method != http.MethodGet {
+		http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
+		return
+	}
+
+	sinceDuration := parseSinceParam(r)
+	projectFilterParam := parseProjectFilter(r)
+
+	projectDir := sourceDir
+	if projectDir == "" || projectDir == "unknown" {
+		projectDir, _ = os.Getwd()
+	}
+
+	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
+
+	untracked, err := listUntrackedSessions(client, projectDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		log.Printf("Warning: failed to list sessions: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	sessionStatusMap := make(map[string]opencode.SessionStatusInfo)
+	if status, err := client.GetAllSessionStatus(); err != nil {
+		log.Printf("Warning: failed to fetch session status: %v", err)
+	} else {
+		sessionStatusMap = status
+	}
+
+	responses := make([]SessionAPIResponse, 0, len(untracked))
+	for _, entry := range untracked {
+		updatedAt := time.Unix(entry.Session.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
+		createdAt := time.Unix(entry.Session.Time.Created/1000, 0)
+		sessionTime := updatedAt
+		if sessionTime.IsZero() {
+			sessionTime = createdAt
+		}
+
+		if !filterByTime(sessionTime, sinceDuration) {
+			continue
+		}
+		if len(projectFilterParam) > 0 && !filterByProject(entry.Session.Directory, projectFilterParam) {
+			continue
+		}
+
+		status := "idle"
+		isProcessing := false
+		if statusInfo, ok := sessionStatusMap[entry.Session.ID]; ok {
+			isProcessing = statusInfo.IsBusy() || statusInfo.IsRetrying()
+			if isProcessing {
+				status = "active"
+			}
+		}
+
+		responses = append(responses, SessionAPIResponse{
+			ID:            entry.Session.ID,
+			Title:         entry.Session.Title,
+			Category:      entry.Category,
+			Role:          entry.Role,
+			BeadsID:       entry.BeadsID,
+			Tier:          entry.Tier,
+			SpawnMode:     entry.SpawnMode,
+			Skill:         entry.Skill,
+			Model:         entry.Model,
+			WorkspacePath: entry.WorkspacePath,
+			ProjectDir:    entry.Session.Directory,
+			Status:        status,
+			IsProcessing:  isProcessing,
+			CreatedAt:     formatSessionTime(entry.Session.Time.Created),
+			UpdatedAt:     formatSessionTime(entry.Session.Time.Updated),
+		})
+	}
+
+	sort.Slice(responses, func(i, j int) bool {
+		return responses[i].UpdatedAt > responses[j].UpdatedAt
+	})
+
+	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
+	if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(responses); err != nil {
+		http.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf("Failed to encode sessions: %v", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)
+		return
+	}
+}

diff --git a/cmd/orch/sessions_untracked.go b/cmd/orch/sessions_untracked.go
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..e27a3460b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cmd/orch/sessions_untracked.go
@@ -0,0 +1,152 @@
+package main
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"sort"
+	"strings"
+
+	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/opencode"
+	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
+)
+
+var (
+	untrackedSessionsJSON    bool
+	untrackedSessionsLimit   int
+	untrackedSessionsProject string
+)
+
+var sessionsCmd = &cobra.Command{
+	Use:   "sessions",
+	Short: "List untracked OpenCode sessions (orchestrator/ad-hoc/no-track)",
+	Long: `List untracked OpenCode sessions.
+
+This command shows sessions that do NOT map to beads-tracked work items:
+- orchestrator sessions (role=orchestrator)
+- ad-hoc sessions (no beads_id)
+- --no-track sessions (explicit opt-out)
+
+Two-lane split:
+  orch status   = tracked work only
+  orch sessions = untracked sessions only
+
+Examples:
+  orch sessions
+  orch sessions --limit 50
+  orch sessions --project orch-go
+  orch sessions --json`,
+	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+		return runUntrackedSessions(serverURL)
+	},
+}
+
+func init() {
+	sessionsCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&untrackedSessionsJSON, "json", false, "Output as JSON")
+	sessionsCmd.Flags().IntVar(&untrackedSessionsLimit, "limit", 25, "Maximum number of sessions to show")
+	sessionsCmd.Flags().StringVar(&untrackedSessionsProject, "project", "", "Filter by project (name or path)")
+
+	rootCmd.AddCommand(sessionsCmd)
+}
+
+type UntrackedSessionOutput struct {
+	ID            string `json:"id"`
+	Title         string `json:"title,omitempty"`
+	Category      string `json:"category"`
+	Role          string `json:"role,omitempty"`
+	BeadsID       string `json:"beads_id,omitempty"`
+	Tier          string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
+	SpawnMode     string `json:"spawn_mode,omitempty"`
+	Skill         string `json:"skill,omitempty"`
+	Model         string `json:"model,omitempty"`
+	WorkspacePath string `json:"workspace_path,omitempty"`
+	ProjectDir    string `json:"project_dir,omitempty"`
+	CreatedAt     string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
+	UpdatedAt     string `json:"updated_at,omitempty"`
+}
+
+func runUntrackedSessions(serverURL string) error {
+	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
+	projectDir, _ := os.Getwd()
+
+	untracked, err := listUntrackedSessions(client, projectDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to list sessions: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	filters := parseProjectFilters(untrackedSessionsProject)
+	filtered := make([]UntrackedSessionOutput, 0, len(untracked))
+	for _, entry := range untracked {
+		if len(filters) > 0 && !filterByProject(entry.Session.Directory, filters) {
+			continue
+		}
+		filtered = append(filtered, entry.toOutput())
+	}
+
+	sort.Slice(filtered, func(i, j int) bool {
+		return filtered[i].UpdatedAt > filtered[j].UpdatedAt
+	})
+
+	if untrackedSessionsLimit > 0 && len(filtered) > untrackedSessionsLimit {
+		filtered = filtered[:untrackedSessionsLimit]
+	}
+
+	if untrackedSessionsJSON {
+		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(filtered, "", "  ")
+		if err != nil {
+			return fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal JSON: %w", err)
+		}
+		fmt.Println(string(data))
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	if len(filtered) == 0 {
+		fmt.Println("No untracked sessions found")
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	fmt.Printf("Found %d untracked sessions:\n\n", len(filtered))
+	for _, session := range filtered {
+		updated := session.UpdatedAt
+		if updated == "" {
+			updated = "unknown"
+		}
+		fmt.Printf("%-22s  %s\n", session.ID, truncate(session.Title, 70))
+		fmt.Printf("  Category: %s | Updated: %s\n", session.Category, updated)
+		if session.ProjectDir != "" {
+			fmt.Printf("  Project:  %s\n", session.ProjectDir)
+		}
+		if session.Role != "" || session.Skill != "" || session.Tier != "" || session.SpawnMode != "" {
+			fmt.Printf("  Meta:     role=%s skill=%s tier=%s mode=%s\n",
+				emptyFallback(session.Role, "-"),
+				emptyFallback(session.Skill, "-"),
+				emptyFallback(session.Tier, "-"),
+				emptyFallback(session.SpawnMode, "-"))
+		}
+		fmt.Println()
+	}
+
+	return nil
+}
+
+func parseProjectFilters(value string) []string {
+	if value == "" {
+		return nil
+	}
+	parts := strings.Split(value, ",")
+	result := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
+	for _, part := range parts {
+		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(part)
+		if trimmed != "" {
+			result = append(result, trimmed)
+		}
+	}
+	return result
+}
+
+func emptyFallback(value, fallback string) string {
+	if value == "" {
+		return fallback
+	}
+	return value
+}
diff --git a/cmd/orch/untracked_sessions.go b/cmd/orch/untracked_sessions.go
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..643e77059
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cmd/orch/untracked_sessions.go
@@ -0,0 +1,133 @@
+package main
+
+import (
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/dylan-conlin/orch-go/pkg/opencode"
+)
+
+type untrackedSession struct {
+	Session       opencode.Session
+	Category      string
+	Role          string
+	BeadsID       string
+	Tier          string
+	SpawnMode     string
+	Skill         string
+	Model         string
+	WorkspacePath string
+}
+
+func listUntrackedSessions(client *opencode.Client, currentProjectDir string) ([]untrackedSession, error) {
+	sessions, err := listSessionsAcrossProjects(client, currentProjectDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+
+	result := make([]untrackedSession, 0, len(sessions))
+	for _, session := range sessions {
+		category, meta := classifyUntrackedSession(session)
+		if category == "" {
+			continue
+		}
+		result = append(result, untrackedSession{
+			Session:       session,
+			Category:      category,
+			Role:          meta.Role,
+			BeadsID:       meta.BeadsID,
+			Tier:          meta.Tier,
+			SpawnMode:     meta.SpawnMode,
+			Skill:         meta.Skill,
+			Model:         meta.Model,
+			WorkspacePath: meta.WorkspacePath,
+		})
+	}
+
+	return result, nil
+}
+
+type untrackedSessionMeta struct {
+	Role          string
+	BeadsID       string
+	Tier          string
+	SpawnMode     string
+	Skill         string
+	Model         string
+	WorkspacePath string
+}
+
+func classifyUntrackedSession(session opencode.Session) (string, untrackedSessionMeta) {
+	meta := untrackedSessionMeta{}
+	if session.Metadata != nil {
+		meta.Role = strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata["role"])
+		meta.BeadsID = strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata["beads_id"])
+		meta.Tier = strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata["tier"])
+		meta.SpawnMode = strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata["spawn_mode"])
+		meta.Skill = strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata["skill"])
+		meta.Model = strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata["model"])
+		meta.WorkspacePath = strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata["workspace_path"])
+	}
+
+	roleLower := strings.ToLower(meta.Role)
+	skillLower := strings.ToLower(meta.Skill)
+	if roleLower == "" && (skillLower == "orchestrator" || skillLower == "meta-orchestrator") {
+		roleLower = skillLower
+		meta.Role = meta.Skill
+	}
+
+	if roleLower == "orchestrator" || roleLower == "meta-orchestrator" {
+		return "orchestrator", meta
+	}
+
+	noTrack := false
+	if session.Metadata != nil {
+		noTrack = parseMetadataBool(session.Metadata["no_track"])
+	}
+	if noTrack {
+		return "no-track", meta
+	}
+
+	if meta.BeadsID == "" {
+		return "ad-hoc", meta
+	}
+
+	return "", meta
+}
+
+func parseMetadataBool(value string) bool {
+	if value == "" {
+		return false
+	}
+	switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(value)) {
+	case "1", "true", "yes", "y":
+		return true
+	default:
+		return false
+	}
+}
+
+func formatSessionTime(epochMillis int64) string {
+	if epochMillis <= 0 {
+		return ""
+	}
+	return time.Unix(epochMillis/1000, 0).Format(time.RFC3339)
+}
+
+func (s untrackedSession) toOutput() UntrackedSessionOutput {
+	return UntrackedSessionOutput{
+		ID:            s.Session.ID,
+		Title:         s.Session.Title,
+		Category:      s.Category,
+		Role:          s.Role,
+		BeadsID:       s.BeadsID,
+		Tier:          s.Tier,
+		SpawnMode:     s.SpawnMode,
+		Skill:         s.Skill,
+		Model:         s.Model,
+		WorkspacePath: s.WorkspacePath,
+		ProjectDir:    s.Session.Directory,
+		CreatedAt:     formatSessionTime(s.Session.Time.Created),
+		UpdatedAt:     formatSessionTime(s.Session.Time.Updated),
+	}
+}
